Joshua20:9 These were the designated cities of refuge. Any Israelite or any foreigner living among them who had accidentally killed someone could run to one of these cities for protection. He could live there safely until he had a fair trial

Psalms63:11 These liars will be silenced forever! But with the anointing of a king I will dance and rejoice along with all his devoted lovers who trust in him.

Psalms72:1 O God
Psalms72:2 Help him to give true justice to your people
Psalms72:3 Then the mountains of influence will be fruitful
Psalms72:4 May the poor and humble have an advocate with the king. May he consider the children of the poor and crush the cruel oppressor.
Psalms72:5 The sun and moon will stop shining before your devoted lovers will stop worshiping; for ages upon ages the people will love and adore you!
Psalms72:6 Your favor will fall like rain upon our surrendered lives
Psalms72:7 In the days of his reign the righteous will spring forth with the abundance of peace and prosperity forevermore.
Psalms72:8 May he subdue and take dominion from sea to sea; may he rule from the river to the rim.
Psalms72:9 Desert nomads are bowing at his feet; every enemy is falling facedown
Psalms72:10 Distant kings will surrender and come with their gifts from every continent and coastland; they will offer their tribute to you.
Psalms72:11 O King of kings
Psalms72:12 He will care for the needy and neglected when they cry to him for help. The humble and helpless will know his kindness
Psalms72:13 
Psalms72:14 They will be rescued from tyranny and torture
Psalms72:15 Long live this King! May the wealth of the world be laid before him. May there be ceaseless praise and prayer to him. May all the blessing be brought to him.
Psalms72:16 Bless us with a bountiful harvest
Psalms72:17 so that his name may be honored forever! May the fame of his name spring forth! May it shine on
Psalms72:18 Praise Yahweh forever
Psalms72:19 The blazing glory of his name will be praised forever! May all the earth overflow with his glory! Faithful is our King! Amen!
Psalms72:20 This concludes the poetry sung by David
Psalms73:1 No one can deny it—God is really good to Israel and to all those with pure hearts. But I nearly missed seeing it for myself.
Psalms73:2 Here’s my story: I narrowly missed losing it all.
Psalms73:3 I was stumbling over what I saw the wicked doing. For when I saw the boasters with such wealth and prosperity
Psalms73:4 Indulging in whatever they wanted
Psalms73:5 
Psalms73:6 They didn’t even try to hide their pride and opulence. Cruelty and violence are parts of their lifestyle.
Psalms73:7 Pampered and pompous
Psalms73:8 They’re such snobs—looking down their noses. They even scoff at God! They are nothing but bullies threatening God’s people.
Psalms73:9 They are loudmouths with no fear of God
Psalms73:10 Yet the people keep coming back to listen to more of their nonsense.
Psalms73:11 They tell their cohorts
Psalms73:12 These are the wicked ones I’m talking about! They never have to lift a finger
Psalms73:13 Have I been foolish to play by the rules and keep my life pure?
Psalms73:14 Here I am suffering under your discipline day after day. I feel like I’m being punished all day long.
Psalms73:15 If I had given in to my pain and spoken of what I was really feeling
Psalms73:16 When I tried to understand it all
Psalms73:17 But then one day I was brought into the sanctuaries of God
Psalms73:18 They’re the ones who are on the slippery path
Psalms73:19 It will be an instant end to all their life of ease; a blink of the eye and they’re swept away by sudden calamity! They’re all nothing more than momentary monarchs—
Psalms73:20 soon to disappear like a dream when one awakes. When the rooster crows
Psalms73:21 When I saw all of this
Psalms73:22 I was so stupid. I was senseless and ignorant
Psalms73:23 Yet
Psalms73:24 You lead me with your secret wisdom. And following you brings me into your brightness and glory!
Psalms73:25 Whom have I in heaven but you? You’re all I want! No one on earth means as much to me as you.
Psalms73:26 Lord
Psalms73:27 Those who abandon the worship of God will perish. The false and unfaithful will be silenced
Psalms73:28 But I’ll keep coming closer and closer to you
Psalms74:1 Are you really going to leave us
Psalms74:2 Don’t forget that we are your beloved ones. Wrap us back into your heart again
Psalms74:3 Turn your steps toward this devastation. Come running to bring your restoring grace to these ruins
Psalms74:4 They have come into the very midst of your dwelling place
Psalms74:5 Now everything is in shambles! They’ve totally destroyed it. Like a forest chopped down to the ground
Psalms74:6 All of the beauty of the craftsmanship of the inner place has been ruined
Psalms74:7 They’ve burned it all to the ground. They’ve violated your sanctuary
Psalms74:8 They boasted
Psalms74:9 We don’t see any miraculous signs anymore. There’s no longer a prophet among us who can tell us how long this devastation will continue.

Psalms74:11 Why don’t you do something? You have the power to break in
Psalms74:12 You have always been
Psalms74:13 It was you who split the sea in two by your glorious strength. You smashed the power of Tannin
Psalms74:14 You crushed the might of Leviathan
Psalms74:15 With your glory you opened up springs and fountains
Psalms74:16 You own the day and the night. Sunlight and starlight call you Creator.
Psalms74:17 The four corners of the earth were formed by your hands

Psalms74:20 Remember your promises to us

Psalms74:23 Never forget what your adversaries are saying. For their rage and uproar rise continually against you. It’s time to stand up to them!

Psalms76:1 God is well known in the land of Judah. He is famous throughout Israel
Psalms76:2 making his home in Jerusalem
Psalms76:3 That’s where he smashes every weapon of war that comes against him. That’s where he uses the broken arrows as kindling for his mighty bonfire.
Psalms76:4 God
Psalms76:5 Even the mightiest of men have been paralyzed by your presence. They were so stunned and lifeless
Psalms76:6 When Jacob’s God roared his rebuke
Psalms76:7 No wonder you are greatly feared! You are the awe-inspiring God! For who could ever stand before your face when your fierce anger burns and live to tell about it.
Psalms76:8 As the earth itself holds its breath in awe before you
Psalms76:9 You arise to punish evil and defend the gentle upon the earth.
Psalms76:10 You have power to transform man’s futile anger into praise. The fury of your enemies only causes your fame to increase.
Psalms76:11 So you’d better keep every promise you’ve ever made to the Awesome One
Psalms76:12 He is famous for breaking the spirit of the powers that be. And the kings of the earth will know him as the Fearsome One!
Psalms77:1 I poured out my complaint to you
Psalms77:2 When I was in deep distress
Psalms77:3 As I thought of you I moaned
Psalms77:4 I can’t get a wink of sleep until you come and comfort me. Now I’m too burdened to even pray!
Psalms77:5 My mind wandered
Psalms77:6 Then I remembered the worship songs I used to sing in the night seasons
Psalms77:7 Would you really walk off and leave me forever
Psalms77:8 Has your well of sweet mercy dried up? Will your promises never come true?

Psalms78:1 Beloved ones
Psalms78:2 A parable and a proverb are hidden in what I say— an intriguing riddle from the past.
Psalms78:3 We’ve heard true stories from our fathers about our rich heritage. We will continue to tell our children and not hide from the rising generation the great marvels of our God— his miracles and power that have brought us all this far.
Psalms78:4 
Psalms78:5 The story of Israel is a lesson in God’s ways. He established decrees for Jacob and established the law in Israel
Psalms78:6 For perpetuity God’s ways will be passed down from one generation to the next
Psalms78:7 In this way
Psalms78:8 By following his ways they will break the past bondage of their fickle fathers
Psalms78:9 Take
Psalms78:10 They didn’t really believe the promises of God; they refused to trust him and move forward in faith.
Psalms78:11 They forgot his wonderful works and the miracles of the past
Psalms78:12 even their exodus from Egypt
Psalms78:13 God split the sea wide open
Psalms78:14 By day the moving glory-cloud led them forward. And all through the night the fire-cloud stood as a sentry of light.
Psalms78:15 In the days of desert dryness
Psalms78:16 
Psalms78:17 Yet they kept their rebellion alive against God Most High
Psalms78:18 In their hearts they tested God just to get what they wanted
Psalms78:19 Like spoiled children they grumbled against God
Psalms78:20 
Psalms78:21 Then God heard all their complaining and was furious! His anger flared up against his people.
Psalms78:22 For they turned away from faith and walked away in fear; they failed to trust in his power to save them when he was near.
Psalms78:23 Still he spoke on their behalf
Psalms78:24 
Psalms78:25 Humans ate angels’ food—the meal of the mighty ones. His grace gave them more than enough!
Psalms78:26 The heavenly winds of miracle power blew in their favor
Psalms78:27 
Psalms78:28 Food fell from the skies
Psalms78:29 He gave them all they desired
Psalms78:30 But before they had even finished
Psalms78:31 
Psalms78:32 Yet in spite of all this
Psalms78:33 So God cut their lives short with sudden disaster
Psalms78:34 When he cared for them they ignored him
Psalms78:35 They remembered that God
Psalms78:36 But their repentance lasted only as long as they were in danger; they lied through their teeth to the true God of the Covenant. So quickly they wandered away from his promises
Psalms78:37 
Psalms78:38 But amazingly
Psalms78:39 He knew that they were made from mere dust— frail
Psalms78:40 How many times they rebelled in their desert days! How they grieved him with their grumblings.
Psalms78:41 Again and again they limited God
Psalms78:42 They forgot his great love
Psalms78:43 They disregarded all the epic signs and marvels they saw when they escaped from Egypt’s bondage. They forgot the judgment of the plagues that set them free.
Psalms78:44 God turned their rivers into blood
Psalms78:45 He sent them vast swarms of filthy flies that sucked their blood. He sent hordes of frogs
Psalms78:46 Grasshoppers consumed all their crops.
Psalms78:47 Every garden and every orchard was flattened with blasts of hailstones
Psalms78:48 Even their cattle fell prey
Psalms78:49 Finally
Psalms78:50 He lifted his mercy and let loose his fearful anger and did not spare their lives. He released the judgment-plagues to rage through their land. God struck down in death all the firstborn sons of Egypt— the pride and joy of each family.
Psalms78:51 
Psalms78:52 Then
Psalms78:53 Safely and carefully God led them out
Psalms78:54 Eventually God brought his people to the Holy Land
Psalms78:55 He drove out and scattered all the peoples occupying the land
Psalms78:56 Yet for all of this
Psalms78:57 Like traitors turning back
Psalms78:58 
Psalms78:59 Enraged with anger
Psalms78:60 God walked away from them and left his dwelling place at Shiloh
Psalms78:61 allowing his emblem of strength
Psalms78:62 God vented his rage
Psalms78:63 Their young men fell on the battlefield and never came back. Their daughters never heard their wedding songs
Psalms78:64 Their priests were slaughtered and their widows were killed before they had time to weep.
Psalms78:65 Then all at once the Almighty awakened as though he had been asleep. Like a mighty man he arose
Psalms78:66 He blasted into battle
Psalms78:67 He rejected Joseph’s family
Psalms78:68 He chose instead the tribe of Judah and Mount Zion
Psalms78:69 There he built his towering temple
Psalms78:70 God also chose his beloved one
Psalms78:71 God prepared David and took this gentle shepherd-king and presented him before the people as the one who would love and care for them with integrity

Psalms81:1 Lord
Psalms81:2 Let the celebration begin! I will sing with drum accompaniment and with the sweet sound of the harp and guitar strumming.
Psalms81:3 Go ahead! Blow the jubilee trumpet to begin the feast! Blow it before every joyous celebration and festival.
Psalms81:4 For God has given us these seasons of joy
Psalms81:5 He has given these feasts to remind us of his triumph over Egypt
Psalms81:6 “I have removed your backbreaking burdens and have freed your hands from the hard labor and toil.
Psalms81:7 You called out to me in your time of trouble
Psalms81:8 “Listen to me
Psalms81:9 Don’t ever be guilty of worshiping any other god but me.
Psalms81:10 I am your only God
Psalms81:11 But my people still wouldn’t listen; my princely people would not yield to me.
Psalms81:12 So I lifted my grace from off of their lives
Psalms81:13 O that my people would once and for all listen to me and walk faithfully in my footsteps
Psalms81:14 Then and only then will I conquer your every foe and tell every one of them
Psalms81:15 Those who hate my ways will cringe before me and their punishment will be eternal.
Psalms81:16 But I will feed you with my spiritual bread. You will feast and be satisfied with me
Psalms82:1 All rise! For God now comes to judge as he convenes heaven’s courtroom. He judges every judge and rules in the midst of the gods
Psalms82:2 “How long will you judges refuse to listen to the voice of true justice and continue to corrupt what is right by judging in favor of the wrong?”
Psalms82:3 “Defend the defenseless

Psalms105:9 
Psalms105:10 His promises have become an everlasting covenant to Jacob
Psalms105:11 He said to them
Psalms105:12 They were very few in number when God gave them that promise
Psalms105:13 They were wandering from one land to another and from one kingdom to another.
Psalms105:14 Yet God would not permit anyone to touch them
Psalms105:15 He said to them
Psalms105:16 So God decreed a famine upon Canaan-land
Psalms105:17 But he had already sent a man ahead of his people to Egypt; it was Joseph
Psalms105:18 His feet were bruised by strong shackles and his soul was held by iron.
Psalms105:19 God’s promise to Joseph purged his character until it was time for his dreams to come true.
Psalms105:20 Eventually
Psalms105:21 Then Joseph was put in charge of everything under the king; he became the master of the palace over all the royal possessions.
Psalms105:22 Pharoah gave him authority over all the princes of the land
Psalms105:23 Then Jacob
Psalms105:24 God made them very fruitful
Psalms105:25 God turned their hearts to hate his people and to deal treacherously with his servants.
Psalms105:26 But he sent them his faithful servant
Psalms105:27 Their command brought down signs and wonders
Psalms105:28 By God’s direction
Psalms105:29 God turned their rivers to blood
Psalms105:30 And the judgment-plague of frogs came in enormous numbers
Psalms105:31 God spoke and another plague was released— massive swarms of flies
Psalms105:32 God rained down hail and flaming fire upon Egypt.
Psalms105:33 Their gardens and vines were all destroyed
Psalms105:34 God spoke
Psalms105:35 picking the ground clean of vegetation and crops.
Psalms105:36 Then God struck down their firstborn sons
Psalms105:37 At last
Psalms105:38 Egypt was relieved at their exodus
Psalms105:39 God spread out a cloud as shade as they moved ahead and a cloud of fire to light up their night.
Psalms105:40 Moses prayed and God brought them quail to eat. He satisfied them with heaven’s bread falling from the sky.
Psalms105:41 He broke open the boulder and the waters poured out like a river in the desert.
Psalms105:42 For God could never forget his holy promise to his servant Abraham.
Psalms105:43 So God brought out his chosen ones with singing; with a joyful shout they were set free!
Psalms105:44 He gave them lands and nations
Psalms105:45 All this was done for them so that they would be faithful to keep the ways of God
Psalms106:1 Hallelujah! Praise the Lord! Everyone thank God
Psalms106:2 Who could ever fully describe your glorious miracles? Yahweh
Psalms106:3 The happiest one on earth is the one who keeps your word and clings to righteousness every moment.
Psalms106:4 So remember me
Psalms106:5 Let me share in the wealth and beauty of all your devoted lovers
Psalms106:6 We have all sinned so much
Psalms106:7 Our fathers who were delivered from Egypt didn’t fully understand your wonders
Psalms106:8 Nonetheless
Psalms106:9 You roared over the waters of the Red Sea
Psalms106:10 You freed them from the strong power of those who oppressed them and rescued them from bondage.
Psalms106:11 Then the waters rushed over their enemies and drowned them all— not one survived.
Psalms106:12 Seeing this
Psalms106:13 Yet how quickly they forgot your miracles of power. They wouldn’t wait for you to act when they were hungry
Psalms106:14 but demanded you satisfy their cravings and give them food! They tested you to the breaking point.
Psalms106:15 So you gave them what they wanted to eat
Psalms106:16 They became envious of Moses and Aaron
Psalms106:17 You split open the earth
Psalms106:18 Fire fell from heaven and burnt up all the band of rebels
Psalms106:19 They made an idol of a calf at Sinai and bowed to worship their man-made statue.
Psalms106:20 They preferred the image of a grass-eating ox to the presence of the glory-filled God.
Psalms106:21 They totally forgot it was you who saved them by the wonders and awesome miracles you worked in Egypt.
Psalms106:22 
Psalms106:23 So you decided to destroy them. But Moses
Psalms106:24 Yet they still didn’t believe your words and they despised the land of delight you gave to them.
Psalms106:25 They grumbled and found fault with everything and closed their hearts to your voice.
Psalms106:26 So you solemnly swore to them that they would all die in the desert.
Psalms106:27 And you scattered their children to distant lands to die as exiles.
Psalms106:28 Then our fathers joined the worshipers of the false god named “Lord of the Pit.” They even ate the sacrifices offered to the dead!
Psalms106:29 All they did made you burn with anger. It made you so angry that a plague broke out among them!
Psalms106:30 It continued until Phineas intervened and executed the guilty for causing judgment to fall upon them.
Psalms106:31 Because of this deed of righteousness Phineas will be remembered forever.
Psalms106:32 Your people also provoked you to wrath at the stream called Strife. This is where Moses got into serious trouble!
Psalms106:33 Because the people were rebellious against you
Psalms106:34 Neither did our fathers destroy the enemies in the land
Psalms106:35 But they mingled themselves with their enemies and learned to copy their works of darkness.
Psalms106:36 They began to serve their gods and bow before their idols. All of this led them away from you and brought about their downfall.
Psalms106:37 They even sacrificed their little children to the demon spirits
Psalms106:38 shedding the innocent blood of their sons and daughters. These dark practices greatly defiled the land and their own souls
Psalms106:39 
Psalms106:40 This is why you were furious. As your anger burned hot against them
Psalms106:41 So you turned them over to the crushing hands of other nations
Psalms106:42 Oppressive enemies subdued them
Psalms106:43 Many times you would have come to rescue them
Psalms106:44 Yet even so
Psalms106:45 
Psalms106:46 Then you caused even their oppressors to pity them and show them compassion.
Psalms106:47 Do it again
Psalms106:48 Blessed be our Lord God forever and ever. And let everyone everywhere say
Psalms107:1 Let everyone give all their praise and thanks to the Lord! Here’s why—he’s better than anyone could ever imagine. Yes
Psalms107:2 So
Psalms107:3 
Psalms107:4 Some of us once wandered in the wilderness like desert nomads
Psalms107:5 Starving
Psalms107:6 Then we cried out
Psalms107:7 He led us out by the right way until we reached a suitable city to dwell in.
Psalms107:8 So lift your hands and thank God for his marvelous kindness and for all his miracles of mercy for those he loves.
Psalms107:9 How he satisfies the souls of thirsty ones and fills the hungry with goodness!
Psalms107:10 Some of us once sat in darkness
Psalms107:11 For we rebelled against God’s Word and rejected the wise counsel of God Most High.
Psalms107:12 So he humbled us through our circumstances
Psalms107:13 Then we cried out
Psalms107:14 His light broke through the darkness and he led us out in freedom from death’s dark shadow and snapped every one of our chains.
Psalms107:15 So lift your hands and give thanks to God for his marvelous kindness and for his miracles of mercy for those he loves!
Psalms107:16 For he smashed through heavy prison doors and shattered the steel bars that held us back
Psalms107:17 Some of us were such fools
Psalms107:18 Sick and feeble
Psalms107:19 Then we cried out
Psalms107:20 God spoke the words “Be healed
Psalms107:21 So lift your hands and give thanks to God for his marvelous kindness and for his miracles of mercy for those he loves!
Psalms107:22 Bring your praise as an offering and your thanks as a sacrifice as you sing your story of miracles with a joyful song.
Psalms107:23 Some of us set sail upon the sea to faraway ports
Psalms107:24 We were witnesses of God’s power out in the ocean deep; we saw breathtaking wonders upon the high seas.
Psalms107:25 When God spoke he stirred up a storm
Psalms107:26 Ships were tossed by the swelling sea
Psalms107:27 
Psalms107:28 Then we cried out
Psalms107:29 God stilled the storm
Psalms107:30 We were so relieved
Psalms107:31 So lift your hands and give thanks to God for his marvelous kindness and for his miracles of mercy for those he loves!
Psalms107:32 Let’s exalt him on high and lift up our praises in public; let all the people and the leaders of the nation know how great and wonderful is Yahweh
Psalms107:33 Whenever he chooses he can dry up a river and turn the land into a desert.
Psalms107:34 Or he can take a fruitful land and make it into a saltwater swamp
Psalms107:35 But he also can turn a barren wilderness into an oasis with water! He can make springs flow into desert lands
Psalms107:36 and turn them into fertile valleys so that cities spring up
Psalms107:37 They can plant their fields and vineyards there and reap a bumper crop and gather a fruitful harvest.
Psalms107:38 God will bless them and cause them to multiply and prosper.
Psalms107:39 But others will become poor
Psalms107:40 For God pours contempt upon their arrogant abuse of power
Psalms107:41 But he raises up the poor and lowly with his favor
Psalms107:42 The lovers of God will rejoice when they see this. Good men are glad when the evil ones are silenced.
Psalms107:43 If you are truly wise
Psalms108:1 My heart
Psalms108:2 Arise
Psalms108:3 Wherever I go
Psalms108:4 Your love is so extravagant
Psalms108:5 Lord God
Psalms108:6 Come to your beloved ones and gently draw us out. Answer our prayer for your saving help. Come with your might and strength
Psalms108:7 Then I heard the Lord speak in his holy splendor
Psalms108:8 
Psalms108:9 
Psalms108:10 But who will bring my triumph into Edom’s fortresses?”
Psalms108:11 Lord
Psalms108:12 Give us a father’s help when we face our enemies. For to trust in any man is an empty hope.
Psalms108:13 With God’s help we will prevail with might and power. And with God’s help we’ll trample down our every foe!
Psalms109:1 God of all my praise
Psalms109:2 while the wicked slander me with their lies. Even right in front of my face they lie through their teeth.
Psalms109:3 I’ve done nothing to them
Psalms109:4 Though I love them
Psalms109:5 They continually repay me with evil when I show them good. They give me hatred when I show them love.
Psalms109:6 Show him how it feels! Let accusing liars be raised up against him
Psalms109:7 
Psalms109:8 Shorten his life and let another replace him!
Psalms109:9 Make his wife a widow and his children orphans!
Psalms109:10 Let them wander as beggars in the street
Psalms109:11 Let the creditors seize his entire estate
Psalms109:12 Let no one be kind to him by showing pity to his fatherless children!
Psalms109:13 May all his posterity die with him! Cut down his family tree!
Psalms109:14 And may all the sins of his ancestors be recorded
Psalms109:15 
Psalms109:16 because he never once showed love or kindness to others
Psalms109:17 Since he enjoyed cursing them
Psalms109:18 Bitterness
Psalms109:19 So smother him now with his own curses as his just reward. This will be the Lord’s punishment upon him and all my lying accusers who speak evil against me.
Psalms109:20 
Psalms109:21 But now
Psalms109:22 I’m so broken
Psalms109:23 I’m slipping down a dark slope
Psalms109:24 All my fasting has left me so weak I can hardly stand. Now I’m shriveled up
Psalms109:25 I’m the example of failure and shame to all who see me. They just walk by me
Psalms109:26 You have to help me
Psalms109:27 Then everyone will know that you have won my victory
Psalms109:28 So let them curse me if they want
Psalms109:29 So let my Satan-like accusers fail! Make them look ridiculous if they try to come against me. Clothe them with a robe of guilty shame from this day on!
Psalms109:30 But I will give my thanks to you over and over
Psalms109:31 For you stand right next to the broken ones as their saving hero to rescue them from all their accusers!
Psalms110:1 Yahweh said to my Lord
Psalms110:2 Messiah
Psalms110:3 Your people will be your love offerings. In the day of your mighty power you will be exalted
Psalms110:4 Yahweh has taken a solemn oath and will never back away from it
Psalms110:5 The Lord stands in full authority to shatter to pieces the kings who stand against you on the day he displays his terrible wrath.
Psalms110:6 He will judge every rebellious nation
Psalms110:7 Yet he himself will drink from his inheritance as from a flowing brook; refreshed by love he will stand victorious!
Psalms111:1 Shout hallelujah to Yahweh! May every one of his devoted lovers hear my passionate praise to him
Psalms111:2 For God’s mighty miracles astound me! His wonders are so delightfully mysterious that they leave all who seek them astonished.
Psalms111:3 Everything he does is full of splendor and beauty! Each miracle demonstrates his eternal perfection.
Psalms111:4 His unforgettable works of surpassing wonder reveal his grace and tender mercy.
Psalms111:5 He satisfies all who love and trust him
Psalms111:6 He reveals mighty power and marvels to his people by handing them nations as a gift.
Psalms111:7 All God accomplishes is flawless
Psalms111:8 They are steadfast forever and ever
Psalms111:9 His forever-love paid a full ransom for his people so that now we’re free to come before Yahweh to worship his holy and awesome name!
Psalms111:10 Where can wisdom be found? It is born in the fear of God. Everyone who follows his ways will never lack his living-understanding. And the adoration of God will abide throughout eternity!
Psalms112:1 Shout in celebration of praise to the Lord! Everyone who loves the Lord and delights in him will cherish his words and be blessed beyond expectation.
Psalms112:2 Their descendants will be prosperous and influential. Every generation of the righteous will experience his favor.
Psalms112:3 Great blessing and wealth fills the house of the wise
Psalms112:4 Even if darkness overtakes them
Psalms112:5 Life is good for the one who is generous and charitable
Psalms112:6 Their circumstances will never shake them and others will never forget their example.
Psalms112:7 They will not live in fear or dread of what may come
Psalms112:8 Steady and strong
Psalms112:9 Never stingy and always generous to those in need
Psalms112:10 But the wicked take one look at a life lived like this and they grit their teeth in anger
Psalms113:1 Hallelujah! Praise the Lord! Go ahead
Psalms113:2 For the glorious name of the Lord is blessed forever and ever.
Psalms113:3 From sunrise-brilliance to sunset-beauty
Psalms113:4 For he rules on high over the nations with a glory that outshines even the heavens.
Psalms113:5 No one can be compared to God
Psalms113:6 He stoops down to look upon the sky and the earth.
Psalms113:7 He promotes the poor
Psalms113:8 He turns paupers into princes and seats them on their royal thrones of honor.
Psalms113:9 God’s grace provides for the barren ones a joyful home with children so that even childless couples find a family. He makes them happy parents surrounded by their pride and joy. That’s the God we praise
Psalms114:1 Many years ago the Jewish people escaped Egypt’s tyranny
Psalms114:2 so that Israel
Psalms114:3 The Red Sea waters saw them coming and ran the other way! Then later
Psalms114:4 The land shuddered with fear. Mountains and hills shook with dread.
Psalms114:5 O sea
Psalms114:6 O mountains
Psalms114:7 Tremble
Psalms114:8 He splits open boulders and brings up bubbling water. Gushing streams burst forth when he is near!
Psalms115:1 God
Psalms115:2 Why should the unbelievers mock us
Psalms115:3 But we know our God rules from the heavens
Psalms115:4 The unbelievers worship what they make— their wealth and their work.
Psalms115:5 They idolize what they own and what they make with their hands
Psalms115:6 
Psalms115:7 
Psalms115:8 
Psalms115:9 So trust in the Lord
Psalms115:10 You
Psalms115:11 Yes
Psalms115:12 The Lord will never forget us in our need; he will bless us indeed! He will bless the house of Israel; he will bless the house of Aaron
Psalms115:13 Yes! He will bless his devoted lovers who bow before him
Psalms115:14 God himself will fill you with more. Blessings upon blessings will be heaped upon you and upon your children from the maker of heaven and earth
Psalms115:15 
Psalms115:16 The heavens belong to our God; they are his alone
Psalms115:17 Dead people cannot praise the Lord
Psalms115:18 
Psalms116:1 I am passionately in love with God because he listens to me. He hears my prayers and answers them.
Psalms116:2 As long as I live I’ll keep praying to him
Psalms116:3 Death once stared me in the face
Psalms116:4 I cried out to the Lord
Psalms116:5 He was so kind
Psalms116:6 So I’ve learned from my experience that God protects the vulnerable. For I was broken and brought low
Psalms116:7 Now I can say to myself and to all
Psalms116:8 God has rescued my soul from death’s fear and dried my eyes of many tears. He’s kept my feet firmly on his path
Psalms116:9 and strengthened me so that I may please him and walk before Yahweh in his fields of life.
Psalms116:10 Even when it seems I’m surrounded by many liars and my own fears
Psalms116:11 
Psalms116:12 So now
Psalms116:13 I will lift up his cup of salvation and praise him extravagantly for all that he’s done for me.
Psalms116:14 I will fulfill the promise I made to God in the presence of his gathered people.
Psalms116:15 When one of God’s holy lovers dies
Psalms116:16 Lord
Psalms116:17 Now I’ll worship you passionately and bring to you my sacrifice of praise
Psalms116:18 I’ll keep my promise to you
Psalms116:19 I will worship you here in your living presence
Psalms117:1 Let everyone everywhere shine with praise to Yahweh! Let it all out! Go ahead and praise him!
Psalms117:2 For he has conquered us with his great love
Psalms118:1 Keep on giving your thanks to God
Psalms118:2 Let all his princely people sing
Psalms118:3 Let all his holy priests sing
Psalms118:4 Let all his lovers who bow low before him sing
Psalms118:5 Out of my deep anguish and pain I prayed
Psalms118:6 Now I know
Psalms118:7 For you stand beside me as my hero who rescues me. I’ve seen with my own eyes the defeat of my enemies. I’ve triumphed over them all!
Psalms118:8 Lord
Psalms118:9 Yes
Psalms118:10 Once I was hemmed in and surrounded by those who don’t love you. But by Yahweh ’s supernatural power I overcame them all!
Psalms118:11 Yes
Psalms118:12 
Psalms118:13 They pushed me right up to the edge
Psalms118:14 Lord
Psalms118:15 The joyful songs I now sing will be sung again in the hearts and homes of all your devoted lovers. My loud shouts of victory will echo throughout the land. For Yahweh ’s right hand conquers valiantly!
Psalms118:16 The right hand of Yahweh exalts! The right hand of Yahweh will never fail.
Psalms118:17 You will not let them kill me
Psalms118:18 Yes
Psalms118:19 Swing wide
Psalms118:20 I have found the gateway to God
Psalms118:21 I will offer all my loving praise to you
Psalms118:22 The very stone the masons rejected as flawed has turned out to be the most important capstone of the arch
Psalms118:23 The Lord himself is the one who has done this
Psalms118:24 This is the very day of the Lord that brings gladness and joy
Psalms118:25 O God
Psalms118:26 Blessed is this one who comes to us
Psalms118:27 For the Lord our God has brought us his glory-light. I offer him my life in joyous sacrifice. Tied tightly to your altar
Psalms118:28 
Psalms118:29 So let’s keep on giving our thanks to God
Psalms119:1 You’re only truly happy when you walk in total integrity
Psalms119:2 What joy overwhelms everyone who keeps the ways of God
Psalms119:3 They’ll never do what’s wrong but will always choose the paths of the Lord.
Psalms119:4 God has prescribed the right way to live: obeying his laws with all our hearts.
Psalms119:5 How I long for my life to bring you glory as I follow each and every one of your holy precepts!
Psalms119:6 Then I’ll never be ashamed
Psalms119:7 I will give my thanks to you from a heart of love and truth. And every time I learn more of your righteous judgments
Psalms119:8 I will be faithful to all that your Word reveals— so don’t ever give up on me!
Psalms119:9 How can a young man stay pure? Only by living in the Word of God and walking in its truth.
Psalms119:10 I have longed for you with the passion of my heart; don’t let me stray from your directions!
Psalms119:11 I consider your Word to be my greatest treasure
Psalms119:12 My wonderful God
Psalms119:13 I speak continually of your laws as I recite out loud your counsel to me.
Psalms119:14 I find more joy in following what you tell me to do than in chasing after all the wealth of the world.
Psalms119:15 I set my heart on your precepts and pay close attention to all your ways.
Psalms119:16 My delight is found in all your laws
Psalms119:17 Let me
Psalms119:18 Open my eyes to see the miracle-wonders hidden in Scripture.
Psalms119:19 My life on earth is so brief
Psalms119:20 I am continually consumed by these irresistible longings
Psalms119:21 Your displeasure rests with those who are arrogant
Psalms119:22 Don’t let them mock and scorn me for obeying you.
Psalms119:23 For even if the princes and my leaders choose to criticize me
Psalms119:24 Your commandments are my counselors; your Word is my light and delight!
Psalms119:25 Lord
Psalms119:26 I’ve poured out my life before you
Psalms119:27 Open up my understanding to the ways of your wisdom
Psalms119:28 My life’s strength melts away with grief and sadness; come strengthen me and encourage me with your words.
Psalms119:29 Keep me far away from what is false; give me grace to stay true to your laws.
Psalms119:30 I’ve chosen to obey your truth and walk in the splendor-light of all that you teach me.
Psalms119:31 Lord
Psalms119:32 I will run after you with delight in my heart
Psalms119:33 Give me revelation about the meaning of your ways so I can enjoy the reward of following them fully.
Psalms119:34 Give me an understanding heart so that I can passionately know and obey your truth.
Psalms119:35 Guide me into the paths that please you
Psalms119:36 Cause my heart to bow before your words of wisdom and not to the wealth of this world.
Psalms119:37 Help me turn my eyes away from illusions so that I pursue only that which is true; drench my soul with life as I walk in your paths.
Psalms119:38 Reassure me of your promises
Psalms119:39 Defend me from the criticism I face for keeping your beautiful words.
Psalms119:40 See how I long with cravings for more of your ways? Let your righteousness revive my spirit!
Psalms119:41 May your tender love overwhelm me
Psalms119:42 I’ll always have an answer for those who mock me because I trust in your word.
Psalms119:43 May I never forget your truth
Psalms119:44 I will observe your laws every moment of the day and will never forget the words you say.
Psalms119:45 I will walk with you in complete freedom
Psalms119:46 When I stand before kings
Psalms119:47 My passion and delight is in your word
Psalms119:48 I long for more revelation of your truth
Psalms119:49 Lord
Psalms119:50 In all of my affliction I find great comfort in your promises
Psalms119:51 No matter how bitterly the proud mockers speak against me
Psalms119:52 Your revelation-light is eternal; I’m encouraged every time I think about your truth!
Psalms119:53 Whenever I see the wicked breaking your laws
Psalms119:54 As I journey through life
Psalms119:55 Throughout the night I think of you
Psalms119:56 All this joy is mine as I follow your ways!
Psalms119:57 You are my satisfaction
Psalms119:58 With all my heart I seek your favor; pour out your grace on me as you promised!
Psalms119:59 When I realize that I’m going astray
Psalms119:60 I give my all to follow your revelation-light; I will not delay to obey.
Psalms119:61 Even when temptations encircle me with evil
Psalms119:62 In the middle of the night I awake to give thanks to you because of all your revelation-light—so right and true!
Psalms119:63 Anyone who loves you and bows in obedience to your words will be my friend.
Psalms119:64 Give me more revelation of your ways
Psalms119:65 Your extravagant kindness to me makes me want to follow your words even more!
Psalms119:66 Teach me how to make good decisions
Psalms119:67 Before I was humbled I used to always wander astray
Psalms119:68 Everything you do is beautiful
Psalms119:69 Proud boasters make up lies about me because I am passionate to follow all that you say.
Psalms119:70 Their hearts are dull and void of feelings
Psalms119:71 The punishment you brought me through was the best thing that could have happened to me
Psalms119:72 The words you speak to me are worth more than all the riches and wealth in the whole world!
Psalms119:73 Your very hands have held me and made me who I am; give me more revelation-light so I may learn to please you more.
Psalms119:74 May all your devoted lovers see how you treat me and be glad
Psalms119:75 Lord
Psalms119:76 Send your kind mercy-kiss to comfort me
Psalms119:77 Love me tenderly so I can go on
Psalms119:78 Shame upon the proud liars! See how they oppress me
Psalms119:79 May all your devoted lovers follow me as I follow the path of your instruction.
Psalms119:80 Make me passionate and wholehearted to fulfill your every wish
Psalms119:81 I’m lovesick with yearnings for more of your salvation
Psalms119:82 I’m consumed with longings for your promises
Psalms119:83 My soul feels dry and shriveled
Psalms119:84 How much longer must I wait until you punish my persecutors? For I am your loving servant.
Psalms119:85 Arrogant men who hate your truth and never obey your laws have laid a trap for my life.
Psalms119:86 They don’t know that everything you say is true
Psalms119:87 They’ve nearly destroyed my life
Psalms119:88 Revive me with your tender love and spare my life by your kindness
Psalms119:89 Standing firm in the heavens and fastened to eternity is the Word of God.
Psalms119:90 Your faithfulness flows from one generation to the next; all that you created sits firmly in place to testify of you.
Psalms119:91 By your decree everything stands at attention
Psalms119:92 Because your words are my deepest delight
Psalms119:93 I can never forget the profound revelations you’ve taught me
Psalms119:94 Lord
Psalms119:95 Even though evil men wait in ambush to kill me
Psalms119:96 I’ve learned that there is nothing perfect in this imperfect world except your words
Psalms119:97 O how I love and treasure your law; throughout the day I fill my heart with its light!
Psalms119:98 By considering your commands I have an edge over my enemies
Psalms119:99 You have given me more understanding than those who teach me
Psalms119:100 You have graced me with more insight than the old sages because I have not failed to walk in the light of your ways.
Psalms119:101 I refused to bend my morals when temptation was before me so that I could become obedient to your Word.
Psalms119:102 I refuse to turn away from difficult truths
Psalms119:103 How sweet are your living promises to me; sweeter than honey is your revelation-light.
Psalms119:104 For your truth is the source of my understanding
Psalms119:105 Truth’s shining light guides me in my choices and decisions; the revelation of your Word makes my pathway clear.
Psalms119:106 To live my life by your righteous rules has been my holy and lifelong commitment.
Psalms119:107 I’m bruised and broken
Psalms119:108 Lord
Psalms119:109 Even though my life hangs in the balance
Psalms119:110 The ungodly have done their best to throw me off track
Psalms119:111 Everything you speak to me is like joyous treasure
Psalms119:112 I have determined in my heart to obey whatever you say
Psalms119:113 I despise those who can’t keep commitments
Psalms119:114 You’re my place of quiet retreat
Psalms119:115 Go away! Leave me
Psalms119:116 Lord

Proverbs1:1 Here are kingdom revelations
Proverbs1:2 Within these sayings will be found the revelation of wisdom and the impartation of spiritual understanding. Use them as keys to unlock the treasures of true knowledge.
Proverbs1:3 Those who cling to these words will receive discipline to demonstrate wisdom in every relationship and to choose what is right and just and fair.
Proverbs1:4 These proverbs will give you great skill to teach the immature and make them wise
Proverbs1:5 For the wise
Proverbs1:6 These kingdom revelations will break open your understanding to unveil the deeper meaning of parables
Proverbs1:7 We cross the threshold of true knowledge when we live in obedient devotion to God. Stubborn know-it-alls will never stop to do this
Proverbs1:8 Pay close attention
Proverbs1:9 For their insight will bring you success
Proverbs1:10 When peer pressure compels you to go with the crowd and sinners invite you to join in
Proverbs1:11 When the gang says— “We’re going to steal and kill and get away with it.

Proverbs31:10 Who could ever find a wife like this one — she is a woman of strength and mighty valor! She’s full of wealth and wisdom. The price paid for her was greater than many jewels.
Proverbs31:11 Her husband has entrusted his heart to her
Proverbs31:12 All throughout her life she brings him what is good and not evil.
Proverbs31:13 She searches out continually to possess that which is pure and righteous. She delights in the work of her hands.
Proverbs31:14 She gives out revelation-truth to feed others. She is like a trading ship bringing divine supplies from the merchant.
Proverbs31:15 Even in the night season she arises and sets food on the table for hungry ones in her house and for others.
Proverbs31:16 She sets her heart upon a field and takes it as her own. She labors there to plant the living vines.
Proverbs31:17 She wraps herself in strength
Proverbs31:18 She tastes and experiences a better substance

Proverbs31:21 She is not afraid of tribulation
Proverbs31:22 Her clothing is beautifully knit together — a purple gown of exquisite linen.
Proverbs31:23 Her husband is famous and admired by all
Proverbs31:24 Even her works of righteousness she does for the benefit of her enemies.
Proverbs31:25 Bold power and glorious majesty are wrapped around her as she laughs with joy over the latter days.
Proverbs31:26 Her teachings are filled with wisdom and kindness as loving instruction pours from her lips.
Proverbs31:27 She watches over the ways of her household and meets every need they have.

Proverbs31:29 “There are many valiant and noble ones
Proverbs31:30 Charm can be misleading
Proverbs31:31 So go ahead and give her the credit that is due

Isaiah1:1 Here is the vision that Isaiah
Isaiah1:2 Listen
Isaiah1:3 Even a dumb ox instinctively knows its owner and the stubborn mule knows the hand that feeds it
Isaiah1:4 Oh
Isaiah1:5 Why would you seek to be injured further? Why would you stubbornly continue in your rebellion? Your whole head is sick
Isaiah1:6 You are corrupt from the bottom of your feet to the top of your head. There is no integrity — nothing but bruises
Isaiah1:7 Your country is devastated and your cities burned to the ground; foreigners plunder your crops before your eyes— with nothing but devastation and destruction in their wake!
Isaiah1:8 And the daughter of Zion is left as helpless as a deserted shack in a vineyard or a flimsy shelter in a field of cucumbers— in every way like a city besieged!
Isaiah1:9 If the Lord of Angel Armies had not left us survivors
Isaiah1:10 Hear the word of Yahweh: “You leaders of Sodom
Isaiah1:11 And Yahweh keeps saying: “Why such countless sacrifices—what use are they to me? I’ve had my fill of your burnt offerings of rams and your fattened animals. I find no delight in the blood of bulls
Isaiah1:12 When you come before my face
Isaiah1:13 Stop bringing your meaningless offerings. Your burning incense stinks! Your sin-stained celebrations
Isaiah1:14 With all my soul I hate your new moon festivals and your feasts; they are nothing but a burden that I’m sick and tired of carrying.
Isaiah1:15 When you stretch out your hands to pray
Isaiah1:16 Wash yourselves and make yourselves clean. Remove your evil actions from my sight and stop sinning!
Isaiah1:17 Learn what it means to do what is good by seeking righteousness and justice! Rescue the oppressed. Uphold the rights of the fatherless and defend the widow’s cause.
Isaiah1:18 Come now and let’s deliberate over the next steps to take together.” Yahweh promises you over and over: “Though your sins stain you like scarlet
Isaiah1:19 If you have a willing heart to let me help you
Isaiah1:20 But if you are stubborn and refuse to obey
Isaiah1:21 Look how the once faithful city has become as unfaithful as a prostitute! She who was once the “Center of Justice
Isaiah1:22 She was once like sterling silver
Isaiah1:23 Your rulers are rebellious and companions of crooks. They are self-centered racketeers who love a bribe and who chase after payoffs. They don’t defend the fatherless or consider the rights of a helpless widow.
Isaiah1:24 Therefore
Isaiah1:25 I will put my fiery hand upon you and purify you with fire into something clean.”
Isaiah1:26 “I will restore deliverers as in former times and your wise counselors as at the beginning. Only then will you be called the Righteous City and the Faithful City!”
Isaiah1:27 Yes
Isaiah1:28 There will be a shattering of rebels and sinners together
Isaiah1:29 You will reap shame from the idols you once delighted in and you will be humiliated by your cultic sacred groves
Isaiah1:30 You will be like an oak tree with faded
Isaiah1:31 The “powerful elite” will become like kindling and their evil deeds like sparks—both will burn together and no one will be able to put out the fire.

Isaiah2:2 In the last days

Isaiah2:6 Lord

Isaiah2:9 The people bow down low before the “no-gods
Isaiah2:10 Enter into the rock and hide in the dust from the dreadful presence of Yahweh and from his majestic glory.

Isaiah2:12 The Lord of Angel Armies has a day of humiliation in store for all the high and mighty
Isaiah2:13 His judgment is coming against all the lofty cedars of Lebanon and all the oaks of Bashan
Isaiah2:14 against all the high mountains and all the lofty hills
Isaiah2:15 against every high tower and every fortress wall
Isaiah2:16 against all the trading ships of Tarshish and all the impressive sailing vessels.

Isaiah2:18 Every worthless idol will utterly pass away.
Isaiah2:19 People will hide in caves and holes in the ground from the dreadful presence of Yahweh and from his majestic glory when he rises to mightily shake the earth.
Isaiah2:20 In that day
Isaiah2:21 as they crawl into the clefts of the rock to hide from the dreadful presence of Yahweh and from the majesty of his glory when he rises to shake the earth mightily!

Isaiah40:21 Don’t you realize that God is the Creator? Don’t you hear the truth? Haven’t you been told this from the beginning? Haven’t you understood this since he laid a firm foundation for the earth?

Isaiah41:4 Who achieved all this and made it happen? Who guides the destiny of each generation from the first until now? I am the one! I am Yahweh

Isaiah60:12 The nation or kingdom that refuses to serve you will perish and be utterly ruined.
Isaiah60:13 Lebanon’s glory will be yours. The cypress
Isaiah60:14 The descendants of your oppressors will come bowing low before you

Isaiah60:16 You will guzzle the milk of nations and suckle at the breast of kings. You will know me intimately that I am Yahweh
Isaiah60:17 “I will replace your copper with gold
Isaiah60:18 Threats of violence will no longer be heard in your land
Isaiah60:19 “The sun will no longer be needed to brighten the day
Isaiah60:20 There will be no more sunsets or new moons
Isaiah60:21 All your people will be righteous and will permanently possess the land. I planted them there as a tender sapling
Isaiah60:22 I will multiply the least of you into a thousand and the weakest one into a mighty nation. I am Yahweh

Isaiah61:3 to strengthen those crushed by despair who mourn in Zion— to give them a beautiful bouquet in the place of ashes

Isaiah65:1 “I revealed myself to those who didn’t even ask to know me. Those who did not seek me found me. I said to a nation that did not call on my name

Isaiah65:3 They are a people who continually insult me to my face. They openly and defiantly offer sacrifices to their gods in their sacred groves and burn incense on pagan altars.

Isaiah66:1 This is what Yahweh says: “The heavens are my throne and the earth is my footstool. Where is the house you will build for me? Where is the place where I will rest?
Isaiah66:2 My hand made these things so they all belong to me
Isaiah66:3 But the one who offers a bull with no humility is like one who kills a man. The one who offers a lamb without contrition is like one who breaks a dog’s neck. The one who brings grain offerings with no heart-purity is like one who offers pig’s blood! The one who offers incense with no sincerity is like one who kisses an idol! They have all chosen their own way
Isaiah66:4 So I chose to punish them and to bring on them what they fear the most because I called out to them and they ignored me. I spoke and they did not listen but did what is evil in my sight and chose what I despise.”
Isaiah66:5 Hear the words of Yahweh
Isaiah66:6 Listen! A sound of uproar is coming from the city a sound from the temple! It is the thunder of Yahweh as he completely punishes his enemies!
Isaiah66:7 Zion gave birth suddenly
Isaiah66:8 Who has ever seen or heard of such a wonder? Could a country be born in a day? Can a nation be birthed so suddenly? Yet no sooner does Zion go into labor than she gives birth to sons!
Isaiah66:9 Yahweh
Isaiah66:10 So rejoice with Jerusalem and be glad for her! All who love her
Isaiah66:11 You
Isaiah66:12 For Yahweh says: “I will extend to her prosperity like a river and the wealth of gentiles like a flooding river. You will nurse from her breast
Isaiah66:13 As a mother tenderly comforts her child
Isaiah66:14 When you feast your eyes on this
Isaiah66:15 Look! Yahweh is coming as a raging fire and his chariots like a whirlwind to unleash his anger with fiery fury and with the fiery lightning of his rebuke!
Isaiah66:16 For with fire and with his sword Yahweh will judge humanity— many will be the slain of Yahweh!
Isaiah66:17 “The end is near for those who ‘consecrate’ and ‘purify’ themselves to enter their cultic groves! They go in procession one after another into their ceremonies. They eat disgusting foods —pork
Isaiah66:18 I despise their evil works and their thoughts
Isaiah66:19 I will set an amazing sign among them. I will send some of the remnant to other nations—to Tarshish
Isaiah66:20 And they will bring back from the nations your own people as an offering to me
Isaiah66:21 And some of them I will appoint as Levitical priests
Isaiah66:22 This is what Yahweh declares: “The new heavens and the new earth that I am making will remain and endure before me in the same way your offspring and your name will endure.
Isaiah66:23 From one Sabbath to the next
Isaiah66:24 “They will go out of the city and see the dead bodies of those who rebelled against me. For their worm will not die and their fire will never go out. And they will be abhorrent to humanity.”
Jeremiah1:1 The prophecies of Jeremiah
Jeremiah1:2 Yahweh began to reveal his message to him in the thirteenth year of King Josiah son of Amon
Jeremiah1:3 Yahweh continued to speak to him throughout the reign of Jehoiakim son of Josiah
Jeremiah1:4 Yahweh spoke these words to me:
Jeremiah1:5 “Before I shaped you in the womb
Jeremiah1:6 I replied: “But Lord Yahweh
Jeremiah1:7 But Yahweh interrupted me: “Do not use your youth as an excuse
Jeremiah1:8 Fear nothing when you confront the people
Jeremiah1:9 Then Yahweh reached out with his hand and touched my mouth and said to me: “Now I have put my words in your mouth.

Jeremiah1:12 “You have seen the vision correctly

Jeremiah1:15 Listen! I

Jeremiah2:2 “Go

Jeremiah2:6 They never stopped to ask themselves

Jeremiah2:17 Israel

Jeremiah10:2 Yahweh says: “Do not adopt the ways of other nations or be terrified by unusual signs in the heavens even though the unbelieving nations are in awe of them.
Jeremiah10:3 For the religious practices of the nations are delusions. They cut down a tree from the forest
Jeremiah10:4 He adorns his idol with silver and gold and takes hammer and nails to secure it so it won’t wobble.
Jeremiah10:5 Their idols are like speechless scarecrows standing in a field; someone must carry them around because they can’t even take a step. So don’t fear them in any way
Jeremiah10:6 Yahweh
Jeremiah10:7 King of the nations
Jeremiah10:8 What wisdom could they glean from a block of wood? They are foolish blockheads
Jeremiah10:9 They cover their idols with beaten
Jeremiah10:10 Only Yahweh is the true and living God
Jeremiah10:11 “Say to them: “ ‘The gods who did not create the heavens and the earth will be destroyed from the earth and from under the heavens.’ ”
Jeremiah10:12 Yahweh made the earth by his glorious power and firmly established it by his great wisdom. By his skill he stretched out the sky.
Jeremiah10:13 When he speaks
Jeremiah10:14 Everyone is ignorant
Jeremiah10:15 What they make is worthless—nothing but a joke! When Yahweh comes to punish them
Jeremiah10:16 You idols are nothing like Yahweh
Jeremiah10:17 Jerusalem
Jeremiah10:18 For Yahweh says: “Look! I am throwing you off the land

Jeremiah10:21 For the leaders of the nation are foolish
Jeremiah10:22 Look
Jeremiah10:23 Now
Jeremiah10:24 Correct us
Jeremiah10:25 Vent your anger and pour it out on all these nations around us. They’re the ones who do not worship you. See how they’ve attacked the people of Jacob

Jeremiah44:1 Here is the word Yahweh gave Jeremiah concerning all the Israelites who were living in Egypt. They were in the cities of Migdol
Jeremiah44:2 “ Yahweh
Jeremiah44:3 This disaster came to your people because of their evil deeds; they have worshiped foreign gods
Jeremiah44:4 Yet I kept sending you my servants the prophets to plead with you to stop doing such detestable things
Jeremiah44:5 But you would not listen or pay attention
Jeremiah44:6 So I let loose a firestorm of my anger
Jeremiah44:7 “And now I
Jeremiah44:8 Why do you keep provoking my wrath by offering incense to Egyptian gods where you have settled? Are you bent on your own destruction? Don’t you realize you will become a curse and a laughingstock for all the nations of the earth?
Jeremiah44:9 Have you forgotten all you have done in Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem
Jeremiah44:10 Even now
Jeremiah44:11 “So
Jeremiah44:12 I will make sure that the remnant of Judah who were determined to live in Egypt will die in Egypt. You will all be killed by the sword or die of starvation

Jeremiah44:14 None of the remnant of Judah who have settled in Egypt will escape or survive to return to the country of Judah. Even though they want to go and live there again

Jeremiah44:19 And besides

Jeremiah44:21 “You and your ancestors
Jeremiah44:22 He could not endure your wicked and disgusting practices any longer. He placed a curse on your land and turned it into a desert without inhabitants as it is today.
Jeremiah44:23 This disaster has plainly overtaken you because you sinned and offered incense to other gods

Jeremiah51:2 I will bring foreigners against Babylon to winnow her and plunder her country. They will attack her on every side in the day of her disaster.
Jeremiah51:3 Shoot down all their archers and all their proud warriors dressed in armor. Do not spare her soldiers! Destroy her entire army!
Jeremiah51:4 The slaughtered will fall throughout the land of the Chaldeans. They will be wounded and die in their city streets.
Jeremiah51:5 Remember
Jeremiah51:6 Flee from Babylon! Save your lives! Don’t you perish when I punish her for her guilt; the time of revenge is here. Now Yahweh will pay her back for what she has done.
Jeremiah51:7 Babylon was like a golden cup in Yahweh ’s hand; she made the whole world drunk. The nations drank her wine and then went mad.
Jeremiah51:8 Suddenly the cup of Babylon falls to the ground and is shattered. Wail for her! Get medicine for her wounds; perhaps she can be cured.
Jeremiah51:9 They said
Jeremiah51:10 Yahweh has judged in our favor. Come

Jeremiah51:13 You who are enthroned beside many rivers and canals and are rich in treasures
Jeremiah51:14 For I have sworn by my great name
Jeremiah51:15 Yahweh made the earth by his glorious power and firmly established it by his great wisdom. His skill stretched out the sky.
Jeremiah51:16 When he speaks
Jeremiah51:17 Idolaters are senseless and know so little. Idol-makers are shamed by what they make since their idols are nothing but lifeless lies.

Jeremiah52:1 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king. He reigned for eleven years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Hamutal daughter of Jeremiah
Jeremiah52:2 He did what was displeasing in the sight of Yahweh
Jeremiah52:3 Yahweh became so angry with the people of Jerusalem and Judah that he banished them from the land. Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.
Jeremiah52:4 In the ninth year of Zedekiah’s reign
Jeremiah52:5 After the city had been surrounded for a year and half
Jeremiah52:6 On the ninth day of the fourth month
Jeremiah52:7 That was when the Babylonians broke through the city wall. That same night
Jeremiah52:8 but the Babylonians pursued King Zedekiah and overtook him in the plains of Jericho. All his army deserted him there and fled.
Jeremiah52:9 The Babylonians captured him and brought him to King Nebuchadnezzar
Jeremiah52:10 Nebuchadnezzar had Zedekiah’s sons slaughtered at Riblah
Jeremiah52:11 Then he put out Zedekiah’s eyes and bound him with chains. Then they dragged him off blinded to distant Babylon and put him in prison till the day of his death.
Jeremiah52:12 In the nineteenth year that Nebuchadnezzar was king of Babylon
Jeremiah52:13 He burned down the temple of Yahweh
Jeremiah52:14 The Babylonians under the command of Nebuzaradan demolished all the walls surrounding Jerusalem.
Jeremiah52:15 Nebuzaradan
Jeremiah52:16 But he also left behind some of the extremely poor people in the land of Judah. They owned nothing
Jeremiah52:17 The Babylonians took everything of value from the temple of Yahweh. They smashed the bronze pillars and confiscated the wheeled stands and the bronze laver. Everything made of bronze they carried off to Babylon.
Jeremiah52:18 They also took the ash pots
Jeremiah52:19 The field general took away every small bowl
Jeremiah52:20 But the two pillars
Jeremiah52:21 Both pillars were identical
Jeremiah52:22 On top of each column was a large section of bronze; its height was over five cubits
Jeremiah52:23 There was a total of one hundred pomegranates on the filigree all around
Jeremiah52:24 Nebuzaradan
Jeremiah52:25 In the city he arrested an official who was in command of the fighting men
Jeremiah52:26 The field general took these men and brought them to the king of Babylon at Riblah.
Jeremiah52:27 At Riblah
Jeremiah52:28 Here is the record of the number of those deported by Nebuchadnezzar in the seventh year of his reign: 3
Jeremiah52:29 In the eighteenth year of Nebuchadnezzar’s reign
Jeremiah52:30 In the twenty-third year of Nebuchadnezzar’s reign
Jeremiah52:31 Thirty-seven years after King Jehoiachin was taken away as a prisoner
Jeremiah52:32 The king of Babylon treated Jehoiachin kindly and gave him a position of honor above the other kings.
Jeremiah52:33 So Jehoiachin laid aside his prison clothes and
Jeremiah52:34 At the command of the king of Babylon
Lamentations1:1 How lonely sits the city once thronged with people! Once great among the nations
Lamentations1:2 She is weeping bitterly through the night
Lamentations1:3 Judah’s people have gone into exile under harsh labor and much pain. They found no place to rest living among foreigners. All their enemies have seized them in the narrow passes.
Lamentations1:4 The empty roads to Zion mourn
Lamentations1:5 Her foes have the upper hand while her enemies are now at ease. For Yahweh has made Jerusalem suffer for her many
Lamentations1:6 All the splendor has departed from the fair daughter of Zion. Her princes are like deer that find no pasture. Weak and weary they stumble
Lamentations1:7 In her days of wandering and affliction
Lamentations1:8 Jerusalem has committed a horrible sin and has become an object of scorn. All who once honored her now despise her
Lamentations1:9 Her filth stains her skirts— she never pictured this outcome. Therefore
Lamentations1:10 The enemy has seized all her precious treasures. Before her very eyes she witnessed foreigners enter her holy sanctuary
Lamentations1:11 Everyone is groaning
Lamentations1:12 “None who passes by even cares. Turn around and look at me! Is there anyone who suffers as much as I? Yahweh
Lamentations1:13 “From on high
Lamentations1:14 “ Yahweh took my sins in his hands and tied them into a yoke. Then
Lamentations1:15 “The Lord has despised and rejected all the mighty warriors who defended me. He mobilized an army to destroy me and crush my young men. The Lord has trampled like grapes in a winepress the fair daughter of Judah.
Lamentations1:16 “This is why I weep bitterly
Lamentations1:17 Zion stretches out her hands for help
Lamentations1:18 “ Yahweh is just to judge
Lamentations1:19 “I called to my lovers

Lamentations2:1 The Lord was so angry that he shrouded the daughter of Zion with shame. He hurled down to the ground the splendor of Israel. He disregarded his footstool on the day of his anger.
Lamentations2:2 The Lord has swallowed up without mercy all the homes of Jacob’s descendants. In his fury
Lamentations2:3 In his burning anger
Lamentations2:4 He bent his bow like an enemy and clenched his fist like a foe. Like an enemy
Lamentations2:5 The Lord became our enemy; he destroyed Israel. He devastated all her palaces and ruined her strongholds. He has multiplied weeping and wailing for Daughter Judah.
Lamentations2:6 He wrecked his temple like a vineyard shack and utterly destroyed his meeting place. Yahweh erased the memory in Zion of his appointed feasts and Sabbaths. In the heat of his anger
Lamentations2:7 The Lord has rejected his own altar and disowned his sanctuary. He has given the walls of her fortresses to her enemies. When the enemy toppled Yahweh ’s temple
Lamentations2:8 Yahweh was determined to destroy the walls of beloved Zion. As builders draw a straight line to build a wall
Lamentations2:9 Her gates have collapsed to the ground; he has wrecked and shattered their bars. Her king and leaders are captives among the gentiles
Lamentations2:10 The elders of beloved Zion sit mute on the ground. They have thrown dust on their heads and put on sackcloth. The young virgins of Jerusalem bow their heads in sorrow to the ground.
Lamentations2:11 My eyes have no tears left
Lamentations2:12 They keep saying
Lamentations2:13 With what can I compare you? What can I say to you
Lamentations2:14 The visions your prophets saw for you were false and deceptive whitewash. They neither exposed your guilt nor brought you back in repentance. The false visions they told you were nothing but delusions.
Lamentations2:15 All who pass your way applaud in mockery. They wag their heads with contempt
Lamentations2:16 Your enemies are a choir of scoffers and scolders. They hatefully sneer and glare at you. They gleefully exclaim
Lamentations2:17 Yahweh has done everything he planned and has carried out his word. As he ordained long ago

Lamentations2:19 Arise
Lamentations2:20 “Look

Lamentations2:22 “You invited terror as if to a festival
Lamentations3:1 I am the man acquainted with misery by the rod of his anger.
Lamentations3:2 He has shepherded me into dark places with no light.
Lamentations3:3 He laid a heavy hand on me
Lamentations3:4 He has made my skin and my flesh waste away; he has broken my bones.
Lamentations3:5 He has besieged and encircled me with a wall of hardship and bitterness.
Lamentations3:6 He has made me sit in darkness like those long dead.
Lamentations3:7 He has walled me in with no way out and weighed me down with heavy chains.
Lamentations3:8 Even when I cry for help
Lamentations3:9 He has made my paths a maze and obstructed my ways.
Lamentations3:10 He has become to me like a bear ready to pounce or a lion lying in wait.
Lamentations3:11 He dragged me away
Lamentations3:12 He has drawn back his bowstring and used me as a target for his arrows.
Lamentations3:13 He shot his arrows deep into my heart
Lamentations3:14 Everyone has made me an object of ridicule; they mock me all day long with their songs.
Lamentations3:15 He has given me my fill of bitterness and made me drunk with wormwood.
Lamentations3:16 He has ground my teeth with gravel and crushed me down to the dust.
Lamentations3:17 You have snatched peace from my soul; I have entirely forgotten your goodness.
Lamentations3:18 I thought to myself
Lamentations3:19 I remember wandering around in misery
Lamentations3:20 Whenever I ponder this
Lamentations3:21 Yet there is one ray of hope when I remember this:

Lamentations3:26 It is always good to hope
Lamentations3:27 It is always good to learn patience and humility from a young age.
Lamentations3:28 If your burden is heavy and hard to bear
Lamentations3:29 Bow low in humble submission

Lamentations3:35 to violate human rights in the presence of the Most High
Lamentations3:36 to cheat someone of justice— these things the Lord does not approve.

Lamentations3:38 Is it not from the decree of the Most High that both calamities and good things come?
Lamentations3:39 Why
Lamentations3:40 Let us examine our path
Lamentations3:41 Let us pray

Lamentations3:43 “You have blanketed us with your anger and then pursued us
Lamentations3:44 You shrouded yourself in a cloud too thick for prayer to pierce.
Lamentations3:45 You have reduced us to rubbish; we are the lowest scum among the nations.
Lamentations3:46 “Our enemies are a choir of scoffers and scolders.
Lamentations3:47 Panic and pitfall have been our portion; calamity and collapse have come upon us.”
Lamentations3:48 My eyes stream with torrents of tears as I weep over the ruin of my beloved people.
Lamentations3:49 My eyes will weep without stopping
Lamentations3:50 until Yahweh looks down from heaven and sees how much we suffer.

Lamentations4:1 How the gold has lost its luster! How the pure gold has lost its value! The sacred stones lie scattered at the corner of every street.
Lamentations4:2 The precious children of Zion
Lamentations4:3 Even the vicious jackal is kind enough to nurse her young
Lamentations4:4 The nursing babies have such thirst that their tongues stick to the roofs of their mouths. Little children ask for bread
Lamentations4:5 Those who used to feast on delicacies now lie dying in the streets. Those who were raised in luxury now huddle in garbage dumps.
Lamentations4:6 The punishment of my beloved people exceeded the judgment of sinful Sodom
Lamentations4:7 Israel’s nobility was once brighter than snow
Lamentations4:8 Now their faces are emaciated; they are not even recognized in the streets. Their skin has shriveled on their bones
Lamentations4:9 Better are those killed by the sword than those killed by famine. They slowly starve to death for lack of food.
Lamentations4:10 The hands of caring women cooked their own children; they became their food when my beloved people were destroyed.
Lamentations4:11 At the peak of Yahweh ’s wrath
Lamentations4:12 No king believed
Lamentations4:13 Yet this happened because of the sins of her prophets and the crimes of her priests
Lamentations4:14 They wandered blindly through the city and were so defiled with blood that no one dared to even touch their clothes.
Lamentations4:15 “Get away! Unclean!” people shouted at them. “Keep back! Keep back! Don’t touch!” So
Lamentations4:16 The face of Yahweh scattered them; he will look on them no more. They had no respect for the priests
Lamentations4:17 We strained our eyes continually and looked
Lamentations4:18 Men stalked us so that we could not walk on our streets. Our end drew near
Lamentations4:19 Our pursuers were swifter than eagles in the sky; they hunted us on the mountains and lay in ambush for us in the wilderness.
Lamentations4:20 They captured Yahweh ’s anointed leader
Lamentations4:21 Go ahead
Lamentations4:22 Your punishment is over
Lamentations5:1 Yahweh
Lamentations5:2 Our inheritance has passed to strangers
Lamentations5:3 We have no fathers; we are orphans
Lamentations5:4 We have to pay for drinking water
Lamentations5:5 Our pursuers are breathing down our necks; we are exhausted and allowed no rest.
Lamentations5:6 We made a pact with Egypt and Assyria just to have enough food to eat.
Lamentations5:7 Our ancestors sinned
Lamentations5:8 Slaves rule over us; there is no one to rescue us from them.
Lamentations5:9 We risk our lives to earn our bread because of armed gangs in the wilderness.
Lamentations5:10 Our skin is scorched from the heat of famine.
Lamentations5:11 Our enemies violated Zion’s women and young girls in the towns of Judah.
Lamentations5:12 Our leaders have been taken and hanged; our elders have received no respect.
Lamentations5:13 Young men are forced into menial labor
Lamentations5:14 The elders no longer give their advice; the young no longer make music.
Lamentations5:15 Joy has vanished from our hearts; our dancing has turned to mourning.
Lamentations5:16 Our joyful celebrations are over. We have sinned
Lamentations5:17 For this is why our hearts are sick and our eyes are veiled with tears
Lamentations5:18 because Mount Zion lies desolate with jackals roaming over it.
Lamentations5:19 Yet you
Lamentations5:20 Have you forgotten us forever? Why have you abandoned us for so long?
Lamentations5:21 Draw us back to yourself
Lamentations5:22 Have you utterly rejected us? Is there no limit to your anger?

Ezekiel1:13 These living creatures looked like burning coals of fire

Ezekiel13:23 Therefore you will no longer conjure up false visions or practice witchcraft. I will rescue my people from your deceptive power. And then you will know without a doubt that I am Yahweh.’ ”

Ezekiel18:1 Yahweh spoke to me again
Ezekiel18:2 “Why do you keep repeating this proverb in the land of Israel: “The parents have eaten sour grapes
Ezekiel18:3 “As I
Ezekiel18:4 Listen
Ezekiel18:5 “Suppose a man is morally good
Ezekiel18:6 He does not take part in the heathen feasts on the mountains nor worship the false gods of the people of Israel. He does not commit adultery nor have sex with a woman during her period.
Ezekiel18:7 He exploits and defrauds no one. He does not steal but generously feeds the hungry and clothes the needy.
Ezekiel18:8 He does not lend at interest or take any profit. He refuses to do anything evil and gives honest judgment between one person and another.
Ezekiel18:9 He keeps my laws and is honest in all that he does. A person like this is righteous
Ezekiel18:10 “But suppose he has a son who is prone to violence and bloodshed
Ezekiel18:11 doing what his father never did. He takes part in the heathen feasts on the mountains
Ezekiel18:12 exploits the poor and needy
Ezekiel18:13 and charges exorbitant interest for profit when lending money—such a person will by no means live. Because he committed all these appalling deeds
Ezekiel18:14 “But suppose this evil man has a son who sees all the sins his father practiced
Ezekiel18:15 and refuses to take part in the heathen feasts on the mountains or worship the false gods of the people of Israel. He does not seduce another man’s wife
Ezekiel18:16 nor exploit nor defraud anyone. He does not steal but feeds the hungry and clothes the needy.
Ezekiel18:17 He refuses to do anything evil and does not lend for profit or charge exorbitant interest. He faithfully keeps my laws and teachings—such a person will certainly live and not die for his father’s sins.
Ezekiel18:18 As for his father
Ezekiel18:19 “Yet you ask
Ezekiel18:20 It is the one who sins who will die. A child is not to bear his father’s guilt nor a father his child’s guilt. Good people will be credited for their own righteousness and the wicked with his own wickedness.”
Ezekiel18:21 “However
Ezekiel18:22 I will not remember any of the sins he committed in the past. Because he has done what is right
Ezekiel18:23 I
Ezekiel18:24 “However
Ezekiel18:25 “But you complain
Ezekiel18:26 When the righteous man abandons his righteous ways
Ezekiel18:27 Similarly
Ezekiel18:28 Having considered his ways and having chosen to renounce all his former sins
Ezekiel18:29 And yet the people of Israel say
Ezekiel18:30 So from here on
Ezekiel18:31 You must get rid of all the guilty sins you have committed and clothe yourselves with a new heart and a new spirit. People of Israel
Ezekiel18:32 I find no pleasure watching anyone die

Ezekiel27:1 Yahweh spoke to me and said
Ezekiel27:2 “Son of man
Ezekiel27:3 Say to Tyre
Ezekiel27:4 Your frontiers were on the high sea
Ezekiel27:5 As if they were building a ship they used cypress from Mount Hermon for all your boards
Ezekiel27:6 They made your oars from oaks of Bashan
Ezekiel27:7 Your sails and banners were of colorful embroidered linen from Egypt. Your deck awnings were made from the finest purple and scarlet cloth from Cyprus.
Ezekiel27:8 Your oarsmen were rulers from the cities of Sidon and Arvad
Ezekiel27:9 The elders and craftsmen of Byblos were those who caulked your seams. “ ‘Ships and sailors the world over came to trade with you.
Ezekiel27:10 Your army was composed of warriors from Persia
Ezekiel27:11 Soldiers from Arvad and Helech manned your walls all around. Brave warriors manned your watchtowers
Ezekiel27:12 “ ‘The people of Tarshish traded with you because of your abundant resources of every kind. They imported your products and exported silver
Ezekiel27:13 The Greeks along with nations of Tubal and Meshech traded with you. They sold you slaves and metal utensils.
Ezekiel27:14 The people of Beth Togarmah traded for your goods
Ezekiel27:15 The people of Rhodes traded with you; many coastal lands were your special customers and paid you in ivory tusks and ebony wood.
Ezekiel27:16 Edom traded with you emeralds
Ezekiel27:17 Judah and the land of Israel also paid for your goods with the finest millet
Ezekiel27:18 Damascus traded with you
Ezekiel27:19 casks of wine from Izalla
Ezekiel27:20 Dedan traded with you in saddle blankets.
Ezekiel27:21 Arabia and all the sheikhs of Kedar were your clients
Ezekiel27:22 Businessmen of Sheba and Raamah traded with you the finest spices
Ezekiel27:23 Haran
Ezekiel27:24 In your marketplace they sold you gorgeous embroidered clothing
Ezekiel27:25 “ ‘Heavily loaded Spanish ships carried your wares in the heart of the seas.
Ezekiel27:26 Your oarsmen rowed you out to the open sea. Stormy east winds wrecked you and sent you to the bottom of the sea.
Ezekiel27:27 On the day of your shipwreck
Ezekiel27:28 When they hear the shouts of your drowning sailors
Ezekiel27:29 All the oarsmen will abandon ship; the seamen and sailors will come ashore.
Ezekiel27:30 They will wail aloud over you and weep bitterly. They will throw dust on their heads in mourning and lie in ashes.
Ezekiel27:31 They will shave their heads in sorrow for you and dress themselves in sackcloth. They will weep bitterly for you
Ezekiel27:32 Grieving
Ezekiel27:33 When you exported your goods to satisfy so many peoples with the abundance of your wealth and wares you enriched the kings of the earth.
Ezekiel27:34 Now you are broken to pieces by the waves; you lie at the bottom of the sea. Your cargo and all your crew have foundered with you.
Ezekiel27:35 Those who dwell on the coast and the islands are stunned at your fate. Horror is written on the faces of their kings
Ezekiel27:36 Your trading partners hiss at you in scorn

Ezekiel39:1 “Son of man
Ezekiel39:2 I will turn you around
Ezekiel39:3 I will knock the bow from your left hand and rip the arrows out of your right.
Ezekiel39:4 You will fall on the mountains of Israel
Ezekiel39:5 Your corpses will lie unburied in the open fields
Ezekiel39:6 “ ‘I will send down fire on Magog and on those living securely on the coastlands
Ezekiel39:7 I will make sure that my holy name is acknowledged among my people Israel
Ezekiel39:8 “ ‘Behold
Ezekiel39:9 “ ‘Then those living in the towns of Israel will go out and gather up all the abandoned weapons of the dead soldiers
Ezekiel39:10 They will not have to gather firewood from the countryside or cut it in the forests since they will be burning all the weapons. They will plunder those who plundered them and take spoils from those who took their spoils. I
Ezekiel39:11 “On that day
Ezekiel39:12 It will take the Israelites seven months to bury them all in order to cleanse the land.
Ezekiel39:13 Everyone in Israel will dig their graves
Ezekiel39:14 And after the seven months are over
Ezekiel39:15 And as they go throughout the country
Ezekiel39:16 near the town of Gog’s Army. After that
Ezekiel39:17 Lord Yahweh said to me
Ezekiel39:18 You will eat the flesh of mighty warriors and drink the blood of the princes of the earth as if they were the best fattened animals from Bashan —rams and lambs
Ezekiel39:19 You will gorge yourselves on fat and drink yourselves drunk on the blood of this sacrifice I am making for you.
Ezekiel39:20 You will be filled at my table
Ezekiel39:21 “I will reveal my glory to the nations. They will witness the way I judge Gog and the display of my power when I punish Gog and his great army.
Ezekiel39:22 The people of Israel will know that I am Yahweh their God from that day forward and forever.
Ezekiel39:23 The nations will also realize that the people of Israel went into captivity because of their guilt
Ezekiel39:24 I treated them as their despicable deeds of infidelity deserved and hid my face from them.”
Ezekiel39:25 So
Ezekiel39:26 They will forget their shame and all their acts of unfaithfulness that they committed against me when they were living safely in their own land with none to make them afraid.
Ezekiel39:27 When I have brought them back from the lands of their enemies
Ezekiel39:28 And they will know that I am Yahweh
Ezekiel39:29 I will pour out my Spirit on the people of Israel
Ezekiel40:1 In the first month of the twenty-fifth year of our captivity
