Ezekiel40:4 The shining man said to me
Ezekiel40:5 Behold
Ezekiel40:6 He went into the opening of the gateway facing east
Ezekiel40:7 Beyond the threshold was a corridor
Ezekiel40:8 Then he measured the porch of the gate on the inside.
Ezekiel40:9 It was eight cubits deep
Ezekiel40:10 On each side of the east gate there were three guardrooms
Ezekiel40:11 He measured the width of the entrance as ten cubits and its length
Ezekiel40:12 And there was a low wall one cubit high in front of the guardrooms. Each of the guardrooms was six cubits square.
Ezekiel40:13 Then he measured the width of the gate from the top back wall of one guardroom to the top back wall of the opposite one. It was twenty-five cubits straight across.
Ezekiel40:14 He calculated the height of the porch

Ezekiel40:28 He then took me into the inner court by the south gate. He measured the south gate
Ezekiel40:29 Its guardrooms
Ezekiel40:30 There were porches all around the gateway and each porch was fifty cubits long and five cubits wide.

Ezekiel41:1 The shining man brought me to the Holy Place and measured its doorframes; each was six cubits wide.
Ezekiel41:2 The width of the entrance was ten cubits
Ezekiel41:3 Then he went inside the innermost chamber and measured the doorframes at the entrance as two cubits
Ezekiel41:4 He measured its length and width as twenty cubits
Ezekiel41:5 He then measured the wall of the temple as six cubits thick. There were side rooms all around the temple that were each four cubits wide.
Ezekiel41:6 And there were three levels of the side rooms
Ezekiel41:7 The rooms were right up to the temple walls. The width of the side rooms increased
Ezekiel41:8 Then I looked down and saw that the temple stood on a raised platform that surrounded it. The platform was a full measuring reed high (six cubits) and provided a foundation for the side rooms.
Ezekiel41:9 The outer wall of the side rooms was five cubits thick. On the raised platform there was a terrace all around the side rooms of the temple. It was twenty cubits between the edge of the platform and the rooms for the priests.
Ezekiel41:10 
Ezekiel41:11 And the doors of the side rooms faced an open area
Ezekiel41:12 The building on the west side of the courtyard was seventy cubits wide. The wall of the building was five cubits thick all around
Ezekiel41:13 Then the shining man measured the length of the temple as one hundred cubits. The length of the courtyard plus the building and its walls was also one hundred cubits.
Ezekiel41:14 The width of the courtyard on the east
Ezekiel41:15 Then he measured the length of the building west of the courtyard
Ezekiel41:16 the thresholds
Ezekiel41:17 On the door to the inner part of the temple and on all the walls all around
Ezekiel41:18 were carved alternating cherubim and palm trees. Each of the cherubim had two faces
Ezekiel41:19 a human face that gazed toward the palm tree on one side and the face of a young lion turned toward the palm tree on the other side. They were carved throughout the temple everywhere.
Ezekiel41:20 The Holy Place had the same pattern of cherubim and palm trees carved on the wall from the floor to the space above the doors.
Ezekiel41:21 The columns of the doorway to the Holy Place were square
Ezekiel41:22 A wooden altar was there
Ezekiel41:23 The Holy Place and the Holy of Holies had double doors.
Ezekiel41:24 Each of the doors had two hinged leaves that swung open in the middle.
Ezekiel41:25 On the doors of the Holy Place were carved cherubim and palm trees just like those carved on the walls. There was a wooden roof over the outside of the doorway to the Holy Place.
Ezekiel41:26 And there were niches with palm trees on either side in the front walls of the temple porch. And the side rooms of the temple also had canopies.
Ezekiel42:1 Then the shining man brought me northward into the outer court and brought me to the rooms opposite the temple courtyard and opposite the outer wall on the north side.
Ezekiel42:2 Along the front
Ezekiel42:3 On one side of the building was the twenty-cubit-wide plaza of the inner courtyard. On the other side was the paved walkway of the outer courtyard. The building itself was three stories high and had balconies.
Ezekiel42:4 And in front of the rooms was an inner passageway
Ezekiel42:5 The top-floor rooms were narrower than those on the middle and bottom stories of the building because the balconies took up some of the space of the lower stories.
Ezekiel42:6 The rooms on the third floor had no columns like the pillars of the outer courtyard. That is why the top story was narrower than the middle and bottom stories. The rooms became smaller the higher they went.
Ezekiel42:7 There was a wall in the outer courtyard
Ezekiel42:8 The wall was like this because the row of rooms along the outer courtyard was fifty cubits long
Ezekiel42:9 Beneath the rooms there was an entrance facing east so that a person could enter them from the outer court. On the south side of the temple

Ezekiel43:14 from the base on the ground to the lower ledge

Ezekiel43:16 and the hearth a perfect square: twelve cubits long by twelve cubits wide.

Ezekiel44:4 Then the shining man led me through the north gate to the front of the temple. And I looked and behold
Ezekiel44:5 And Yahweh said to me
Ezekiel44:6 And tell the rebels among the people of Israel
Ezekiel44:7 You have desecrated my temple by admitting gentiles into my sanctuary who are uncircumcised in heart and body
Ezekiel44:8 Instead of keeping charge of my holy things yourselves
Ezekiel44:9 Lord Yahweh says
Ezekiel44:10 For the Levites who abandoned me when Israel turned their backs on me to worship their false gods must bear the consequences of their own sin.
Ezekiel44:11 They may serve me in my sanctuary by being temple gatekeepers and temple servants. They may kill the animals the people bring to offer as burnt offerings and sacrifices for the people and by serving the worshipers in the temple.
Ezekiel44:12 But because they assisted the people of Israel in the worship of their idols and thereby caused them to sin
Ezekiel44:13 They will never draw near to me again to fulfill the priestly office in my presence
Ezekiel44:14 I will
Ezekiel44:15 Yahweh says
Ezekiel44:16 They will enter my sanctuary and approach my table to minister to me and to oversee the temple worship.
Ezekiel44:17 “When the Zadokite priests enter the gates of the inner court
Ezekiel44:18 Their turbans and undergarments must all likewise be of linen. They are not to wear anything that makes them perspire.
Ezekiel44:19 When they finish their sacred duties
Ezekiel44:20 “The priests must not shave their heads nor let their hair grow long
Ezekiel44:21 “Priests must not drink wine before they enter the inner court.
Ezekiel44:22 “They may not marry a widow or a divorced woman but only an Israelite virgin. They may
Ezekiel44:23 “They must teach my people the difference between what is holy and what is ordinary and make them understand the difference between what is ritually clean for God’s service and what is ritually unacceptable.
Ezekiel44:24 “If a dispute arises
Ezekiel44:25 “A priest must not defile himself by going near a dead person; however
Ezekiel44:26 After his purification
Ezekiel44:27 And the first day he goes back into the inner court to minister in the Holy Place
Ezekiel44:28 Yahweh says of the priests
Ezekiel44:29 “Their food will be the grain offering
Ezekiel44:30 All the best fruits of every kind and all the offerings that the people give to God must go to the priests. And you must also give the first portion of the grain you grind into flour to the priests so that a blessing may rest on your house.
Ezekiel44:31 Priests must not eat any bird or animal that has died a natural death or that wild animals have killed.”

Daniel1:1 It was the third year of the reign of Jehoiakim king of Judah when Nebuchadnezzar
Daniel1:2 My Lord gave Nebuchadnezzar victory over King Jehoiakim and permitted the Babylonian ruler to remove some of the sacred vessels belonging to the temple of the true God. Nebuchadnezzar carried them away to the land of Babylon and placed the sacred vessels into the temple treasury of his own gods.
Daniel1:3 Later
Daniel1:4 who would be suitable for service in the king’s palace. They were to be handsome young men without defect
Daniel1:5 The king provided them a daily allowance of the best food and wine from the king’s table. For three years they were to receive a thorough education
Daniel1:6 Among these men of Judah were Daniel
Daniel1:7 But the chief official of the palace gave them Babylonian names: Daniel he called Belteshazzar
Daniel1:8 However
Daniel1:9 Then God moved on the heart of the chief official to show Daniel favor and compassion.
Daniel1:10 But he warned Daniel
Daniel1:11 Daniel then appealed to the overseer whom the chief officer put in charge over Daniel
Daniel1:12 “Test us
Daniel1:13 Then afterward
Daniel1:14 So the overseer agreed to do what they requested and tested them for ten days.
Daniel1:15 And when the ten days were completed
Daniel1:16 So the guard replaced their diet of royal food and wine with vegetables and water.
Daniel1:17 God imparted insight and skill in every aspect of literature and science to the four Hebrew men. Daniel also had the unique gift of interpreting every kind of supernatural vision and dream.
Daniel1:18 At the end of the period that the king had assigned for their training
Daniel1:19 The king interviewed them all
Daniel1:20 And when the king questioned them on any point of wisdom or understanding
Daniel1:21 And Daniel continued there in Babylon as a royal adviser until the time Cyrus became king.
Daniel2:1 In the second year of his reign
Daniel2:2 So the king called for his magicians
Daniel2:3 He told them
Daniel2:4 The wise men answered him in Aramaic: “May you live forever
Daniel2:5 “My decision is final
Daniel2:6 But if you can tell me what I dreamed and its interpretation
Daniel2:7 They again replied
Daniel2:8 But the king retorted
Daniel2:9 If you do not interpret my dream for me
Daniel2:10 The wise men answered the king
Daniel2:11 Your Majesty
Daniel2:12 At this the king flew into a violent rage and ordered all the wise men of Babylon to be executed.
Daniel2:13 When the king’s decree was issued to have all the wise men killed
Daniel2:14 As Arioch
Daniel2:15 this royal official and asked him
Daniel2:16 Daniel immediately went off to plead with the king for more time so that he could seek the interpretation of the king’s dream.
Daniel2:17 Daniel then went home and explained everything to his friends Hananiah
Daniel2:18 He urged them to beg the God of heaven for mercy to help them unravel the mysterious secret of the king’s dream so that Daniel and his friends would not be executed with the other Babylonian wise men.
Daniel2:19 That night
Daniel2:20 saying
Daniel2:21 He alone controls the changes of times and seasons. He enthrones and dethrones kings
Daniel2:22 He alone reveals deep secrets and mysteries and knows what lies in darkness
Daniel2:23 You are the God worshiped by all my ancestors; I give you thanks and praise
Daniel2:24 Then Daniel went back to see Arioch
Daniel2:25 Without wasting a moment
Daniel2:26 The king said to Daniel (whose Babylonian name was Belteshazzar)
Daniel2:27 Daniel replied
Daniel2:28 but there is an all-knowing God in heaven who reveals the deepest mysteries. Your Majesty
Daniel2:29 “Your Majesty
Daniel2:30 This mystery has been revealed to me not because I am wiser than anyone else but for one divine purpose: so that you
Daniel2:31 “Your Majesty
Daniel2:32 The head of this statue was of purest gold
Daniel2:33 its legs of iron
Daniel2:34 While you were staring at this statue
Daniel2:35 Then
Daniel2:36 This was your dream. And now
Daniel2:37 “Your Majesty
Daniel2:38 He has handed over to you people
Daniel2:39 “And after you are gone
Daniel2:40 “And there will be a fourth kingdom
Daniel2:41 “The feet and toes you saw
Daniel2:42 That its feet and toes were partly iron and partly clay means that this kingdom will be both strong and fragile.
Daniel2:43 And just as you saw the iron and the clay mingled together
Daniel2:44 “In the days of those kings of iron and clay
Daniel2:45 This is the meaning of what you saw in your vision: a rock cut out of a mountain—but not by human hands—a rock that shattered the iron
Daniel2:46 Then King Nebuchadnezzar bowed low to the ground in awe of Daniel. Then he gave orders for incense to be burned and a sacrifice of grain to be offered in Daniel’s honor.
Daniel2:47 The king said to Daniel
Daniel2:48 Then the king promoted Daniel and made him great in the kingdom and gave him many wonderful gifts. He also made him ruler over the whole province of Babylon and the chief of all the wise men of Babylon.
Daniel2:49 At Daniel’s request
Daniel3:1 King Nebuchadnezzar had a golden statue made that was sixty cubits high and six cubits wide. He erected the statue on the plain of Dura in the province of Babylon.
Daniel3:2 He then ordered an assembly of his princes
Daniel3:3 So all these officials gathered in front of the golden statue that the king had made.

Joel1:1 These are the words of Yahweh that came to Joel son of Pethuel.
Joel1:2 Listen to this
Joel1:3 Tell your children about it! And tell them to tell their children and to tell their children to tell the next generation.
Joel1:4 What the cutting-locust has left
Joel1:5 Wake up
Joel1:6 For a nation has invaded my land
Joel1:7 They have completely destroyed my vineyards and reduced my fig trees to splinters; they have stripped off the bark and thrown it away
Joel1:8 Lament like a young bride-to-be dressed in sackcloth for the bridegroom of her youth.
Joel1:9 Yahweh ’s ministers
Joel1:10 The fields are stripped bare
Joel1:11 Be ashamed
Joel1:12 The vine has withered
Joel1:13 You priests
Joel1:14 Declare a holy fast. Call a solemn assembly. Gather the leaders and all the people of the land to the house of Yahweh your God. Cry out with all your hearts to Yahweh!
Joel1:15 How awful that time will be! Look! The day of Yahweh is certainly coming— the day when the Divine Destroyer brings destruction.
Joel1:16 Has not the food supply been cut off before our very eyes? Have not the joyful celebrations been silenced in the temple of our God?
Joel1:17 The seeds shrivel under the dry clods. The storehouses are empty; the barns are bare and broken down because the harvest has disappeared.
Joel1:18 How loudly the cattle groan because they have no pasture! The herds wander bewildered. Not even the flocks of sheep are able to graze.
Joel1:19 Yahweh
Joel1:20 The sources of water have run dry and fire has devoured the pasturelands

Joel3:1 “Behold! In those days
Joel3:2 I will gather all the nations together. I will bring them to the Valley of Yahweh ’s Judgment. I will take them to court there for all they have done against Israel— my people and my inheritance. I will execute judgment upon them because they have scattered my people among the nations and divided my land among themselves.
Joel3:3 They cast lots for my people and sold them into slavery. They traded a boy to pay for a prostitute and trafficked a girl for a drink of wine.
Joel3:4 And what have you done to me
Joel3:5 For you have stolen my silver and gold and carried off my valuable treasures into your temples.
Joel3:6 You have sold the people of Judah and Jerusalem to the Greeks to be taken as slaves far from their homeland.
Joel3:7 Behold! I will rouse them to return and come back from the places to which you have sold them. I will do to you the very thing that you have done to them.
Joel3:8 Your sons and daughters will be sold as slaves to the sons of Judah

Amos1:3 Here is the message of Yahweh: “For the three terrible crimes of the people of Damascus— no

Amos1:7 I will send fire down on the walls of Gaza to consume its fortresses.

Amos1:10 So I will send fire down on the city walls of Tyre to devour her fortresses.”
Amos1:11 Yahweh says this: “For the three terrible crimes of the Edomites— no
Amos1:12 I will set the city of Teman on fire to burn up the fortresses of Bozrah.”

Amos1:14 Therefore I will burn down the city walls of Rabbah to consume its fortresses. War cries will be heard on the day of battle; like a whirlwind
Amos1:15 Ammon’s king will be taken into captivity

Amos2:4 Yahweh says this: “For the three terrible crimes of Judah— no
Amos2:5 I will send fire down on Judah to devour the fortresses of Jerusalem.”
Amos2:6 Yahweh says this: “For the three terrible crimes of Israel— no
Amos2:7 They trample the poor and helpless into the dirt and shove aside the rights of the destitute. A son and his father have sex with the same woman

Amos2:9 “Yet it was I who destroyed the Amorites before your very eyes. They stood as tall as cedar trees and as strong as oaks. I destroyed them root and branch.
Amos2:10 It was I who brought you up from Egypt and led you for forty years through the desert to possess the land of the Amorites.

Obadiah1:12 Do not gloat over your relatives on the day of their misfortune. Do not rejoice over the people of Judah on the day of their ruin. Do not boast that they suffered such anguish.
Obadiah1:13 You should not have entered my people’s city on their day of calamity. You should not have feasted your eyes on their suffering on their day of misery. You should not have seized their wealth on their day of sorrow.
Obadiah1:14 You should not have waited at the crossroads to cut off those who tried to escape. Why did you hand over their survivors on the day of their distress?”
Obadiah1:15 “The day of Yahweh is drawing near for all the nations. As you have done to others

Obadiah1:20 The company of exiles from among the people of Israel will possess the land of the Canaanites as far as Zarephath while the exiles from Jerusalem now in Sardis will occupy the cities of southern Judah.
Obadiah1:21 Victorious deliverers will go out from Mount Zion to rule over the land of Edom

Jonah3:7 He then issued this proclamation: “People of Nineveh
Jonah3:8 Each of you must cover yourself and your livestock with burlap and call on God with all your might. Everyone must turn away from his or her evil ways and violent behavior.

Micah2:1 Disaster is ahead for those who plot evil. They’re as good as dead! Disaster is ahead for those who scheme as they lie on their bed. No sooner is it dawn than they act on their plan just because they can.
Micah2:2 They covet someone’s field and seize it for themselves

Habakkuk1:2 Yahweh

Habakkuk1:9 Their armies invade
Habakkuk1:10 They are a people who mock kings and scoff at those in authority. They laugh at every stronghold; they pile up earthen siege ramps and capture them.

Habakkuk1:12 Yahweh
Habakkuk1:13 Your eyes are so pure that you cannot stand the sight of evil; nor do you look on wickedness with favor. Why then do you tolerate the treacherous? Why are you so silent while sinners devour those more righteous than themselves?

Matthew23:5 Everything they do is done for show and to be noticed by others. They want to be seen as holy

Matthew23:27 “Great sorrow awaits you religious scholars and Pharisees—frauds and imposters! You are nothing more than tombs painted over with white paint—tombs that look shining and beautiful on the outside but filled with rotting corpses on the inside.

Mark4:1 Once again Jesus went to teach the people at the shore of Lake Galilee and a massive crowd surrounded him. The crowd was so huge that he had to get into a boat and teach the people from there.
Mark4:2 He taught them many things by using parables to illustrate spiritual truths
Mark4:3 “Consider this: A sower went out to sow.
Mark4:4 As he sowed some fell along the beaten path and soon the birds came and ate it.
Mark4:5 Some fell onto gravel with no topsoil and quickly sprouted since the soil had no depth.
Mark4:6 But when the days grew hot
Mark4:7 Some fell among the thorns
Mark4:8 But some fell onto good
Mark4:9 If you understand this
Mark4:10 Afterward
Mark4:11 He said to them
Mark4:12 “For even when they see what I do
Mark4:13 Then he said to them
Mark4:14 Let me explain: The farmer sows the message of the kingdom.
Mark4:15 What falls on the beaten path represents those who hear the message
Mark4:16 And what is sown on gravel represents those who hear the message and receive it joyfully
Mark4:17 but because their hearts fail to sink a deep root
Mark4:18 And what is sown among thorns represents those who hear the message
Mark4:19 but they allow the cares of this life and the seduction of wealth and the desires for other things to crowd out and choke the message so that it produces nothing.
Mark4:20 “But what is sown on good soil represents those who open their hearts to receive the message and their lives bear good fruit—some yield a harvest of thirty
Mark4:21 He also gave them this parable: “No one lights a lamp only to place it under a basket or under the bed. It is meant to be placed on a lampstand.
Mark4:22 For there is nothing that is hidden that won’t be disclosed
Mark4:23 If you understand what I’m saying
Mark4:24 Then he said to them
Mark4:25 For those who listen with open hearts will receive more revelation. But those who don’t listen with open hearts will lose what little they think they have!”
Mark4:26 Jesus also told them this parable: “God’s kingdom realm is like someone spreading seed on the ground.
Mark4:27 He goes to bed and gets up
Mark4:28 All by itself it sprouts
Mark4:29 Then
Mark4:30 And he told them this parable: “How can I describe God’s kingdom realm? Let me illustrate it with this parable.

Mark4:32 yet when it springs up and grows

Luke12:15 Speaking to the people

Luke12:17 He thought
Luke12:18 I know what I’ll do! I’ll tear down the barns and build one massive barn to hold all my grain and goods.
Luke12:19 Then I can just sit back
Luke12:20 “God said to him
Luke12:21 The same thing will happen to all those who fill up their lives with everything but God.”
Luke12:22 Jesus taught his disciples
Luke12:23 For your life is infinitely more than just the food you eat or the clothing you wear.
Luke12:24 Take the carefree birds as your example. Do you ever see them worry? They neither grow their own food nor put it in a storehouse for later. Yet God takes care of every one of them
Luke12:25 “Does worry add anything to your life? Can it add one more year
Luke12:26 So if worrying adds nothing
Luke12:27 “Think about the lilies. They grow and become beautiful
Luke12:28 If God can clothe the fields and meadows with grass and flowers
Luke12:29 I repeat: Don’t let worry enter your life. Live above the anxious cares about your personal needs.
Luke12:30 People everywhere seem to worry about making a living
Luke12:31 As you passionately seek his kingdom
Luke12:32 So don’t ever be afraid
Luke12:33 “Now
Luke12:34 Where you deposit your treasure
Luke12:35 “Be prepared for action at a moment’s notice.
Luke12:36 Be like servants anticipating their master’s return from a wedding celebration. They are ready at a moment’s notice to unlock and open the door for him.
Luke12:37 What great joy is ahead for those who stay awake and wait for their Master’s return! He himself will become their servant and wait on them.
Luke12:38 He may appear at midnight or even later
Luke12:39 Of course
Luke12:40 So keep being alert and ready at all times. For I promise you that the Son of Man will surprise you and appear when you don’t expect him.”

Luke12:48 Every servant who does not know his master’s will and unwittingly does what is wrong will receive a less severe punishment. For those who have received a greater revelation from their master are required a greater obedience. And those who have been entrusted with great responsibility will be held more responsible to their master.”

Luke16:2 So the master called him in and said
Luke16:3 “The manager thought
Luke16:4 I have an idea that will secure my future. It will win me favor and secure friends who can take care of me and help me when I get fired!’
Luke16:5 “So the dishonest manager hatched his scheme. He went to everyone who owed his master money
Luke16:6 One debtor owed twenty thousand dollars
Luke16:7

Luke16:10 “The one who faithfully manages the little he has been given will be promoted and trusted with greater responsibilities. But those who cheat with the little they have been given will not be considered trustworthy to receive more.
Luke16:11 If you have not handled the riches of this world with integrity
Luke16:12 And if you’ve not proven yourself faithful with what belongs to another
Luke16:13 It is impossible for a person to serve two masters at the same time. You will be forced to love one and reject the other. One master will be despised and the other will have your loyal devotion. Your choice between God and the wealth of this world is no different. You must enthusiastically love one and definitively reject the other.”

Acts28:9 When the people of the island heard about this miracle
Acts28:10 The islanders honored us greatly
Acts28:11 After three months we put out to sea on an Egyptian ship from Alexandria that had wintered at the island. The ship had carved on its prow as its emblem the “Heavenly Twins.”
Acts28:12 When we landed at Syracuse
Acts28:13 From there we set sail for the Italian city of Rhegium. The day after we landed
Acts28:14 There we found some believers
Acts28:15 When the believers were alerted we were coming
Acts28:16 When we finally entered Rome
Acts28:17 After three days Paul called together all the prominent members of the Jewish community of Rome. When they had all assembled
Acts28:18 After hearing my case
Acts28:19 When the Jews objected to this
Acts28:20 This
Acts28:21 They replied
Acts28:22 But we are anxious to hear you present your views regarding this Christian sect we’ve been hearing about

Acts28:24 Some were converted
Acts28:25 still unable to agree among themselves. They were about to leave when Paul made one last statement to them: “The Holy Spirit stated it well when he spoke to your ancestors through the prophet Isaiah:
Acts28:26 ‘I send you to this people to say to them
Acts28:27 For your hearts are hard and insensitive to me—you must be hard of hearing! For you’ve closed your eyes so that you won’t be troubled by the truth
Acts28:28 “So listen well. This wonderful salvation given by God is now being presented to the non-Jewish nations
Acts28:29 Paul lived two more years in Rome
Acts28:30 He continued to proclaim to all the truths of God’s kingdom realm
Romans1:1 Paul
Romans1:2 My commission is to preach the good news. Yet it is not entirely new
Romans1:3 For the gospel is all about God’s Son. As a man he descended from David’s royal lineage
Romans1:4 but as the mighty Son of God he was raised from the dead and miraculously set apart with a display of triumphant power supplied by the Spirit of Holiness. And now Jesus is our Lord and our Messiah.

Romans1:13 So

Romans1:17 This gospel unveils a continual revelation of God’s righteousness—a perfect righteousness given to us when we believe. And it moves us from receiving life through faith
Romans1:18 For God in heaven unveils his holy anger breaking forth against every form of sin

Romans2:2 We know that God’s judgment falls upon those who practice these things. God is always right
Romans2:3 And no matter who you think you are
Romans2:4 Do the riches of his extraordinary kindness make you take him for granted and despise him? Haven’t you experienced how kind and understanding he has been to you? Don’t mistake his tolerance for acceptance. Do you realize that all the wealth of his extravagant kindness is meant to melt your heart and lead you into repentance?

Romans2:22 You are swift to tell others

Romans2:25 You trust in the covenant sign of circumcision
Romans2:26 And if the uncircumcised one faithfully keeps the law
Romans2:27 And won’t the one who has never had the knife cut his foreskin be your judge when you break the law?
Romans2:28 You are not a Jew if it’s only superficial—for it’s more than the surgical cut of a knife that makes you Jewish.
Romans2:29 But you are Jewish because of the inward act of spiritual circumcision—a radical change that lays bare your heart. It’s not by the principle of the written code

Romans4:1 Let me use Abraham as an example. It is clear that humanly speaking
Romans4:2 Was it by his good works of keeping the law? No. For if it was by the things he did
Romans4:3 Listen to what the Scriptures say: Because Abraham believed God’s words
Romans4:4 When people work
Romans4:5 But no one earns God’s righteousness. It can only be transferred when we no longer rely on our own works
Romans4:6 Even King David himself speaks to us regarding the complete wholeness that comes inside a person when God’s powerful declaration of righteousness is heard over our life. Apart from our works
Romans4:7 Here’s what David says: What happy fulfillment is ahead for those whose rebellion has been forgiven and whose sins are covered by blood.
Romans4:8 What happy progress comes to them when they hear the Lord speak over them
Romans4:9 Now
Romans4:10 How did he receive this gift of righteousness? Was he circumcised at the time God accepted him
Romans4:11 It was later that he received the external sign of circumcision as a seal to confirm that God had already transferred his righteousness to him by faith
Romans4:12 Yes
Romans4:13 God promised Abraham and his descendants that they would have an heir who would reign over the world. This royal promise was not fulfilled because Abraham kept all the law
Romans4:14 For if keeping the law earns the inheritance
Romans4:15 For the law provokes punishment
Romans4:16 The promise depends on faith so that it can be experienced as a grace-gift
Romans4:17 That’s what the Scripture means when it says: “I have made you the father of many nations.” He is our example and father
Romans4:18 Against all odds

Romans4:20 He never stopped believing God’s promise
Romans4:21 
Romans4:22 So now you can see why Abraham’s faith was credited to his account as righteousness before God.

Romans8:1 So now the case is closed. There remains no accusing voice of condemnation against those who are joined in life-union with Jesus
Romans8:2 For the “law” of the Spirit of life flowing through the anointing of Jesus has liberated us from the “law” of sin and death.
Romans8:3 For God achieved what the law was unable to accomplish
Romans8:4 So now every righteous requirement of the law can be fulfilled through the Anointed One living his life in us. And we are free to live
Romans8:5 Those who are motivated by the flesh only pursue what benefits themselves. But those who live by the impulses of the Holy Spirit are motivated to pursue spiritual realities.

Romans8:7 In fact

Romans8:19 The entire universe is standing on tiptoe
Romans8:20 For against its will the universe itself has had to endure the empty futility resulting from the consequences of human sin. But now
Romans8:21 all creation longs for freedom from its slavery to decay and to experience with us the wonderful freedom coming to God’s children.
Romans8:22 To this day we are aware of the universal agony and groaning of creation
Romans8:23 And it’s not just creation. We who have already experienced the firstfruits of the Spirit also inwardly groan as we passionately long to experience our full status as God’s sons and daughters—including our physical bodies being transformed.
Romans8:24 For this is the hope of our salvation. But hope means that we must trust and wait for what is still unseen. For why would we need to hope for something we already have?
Romans8:25 So because our hope is set on what is yet to be seen
Romans8:26 And in a similar way
Romans8:27 God
Romans8:28 So we are convinced that every detail of our lives is continually woven together for good
Romans8:29 For he knew all about us before we were born and he destined us from the beginning to share the likeness of his Son. This means the Son is the oldest among a vast family of brothers and sisters who will become just like him.
Romans8:30 Having determined our destiny ahead of time
Romans8:31 So
Romans8:32 For God has proved his love by giving us his greatest treasure
Romans8:33 Who then would dare to accuse those whom God has chosen in love to be his? God himself is the judge who has issued his final verdict over them—“Not guilty!”
Romans8:34 Who then is left to condemn us? Certainly not Jesus
Romans8:35 Who could ever divorce us from the endless love of God’s Anointed One? Absolutely no one! For nothing in the universe has the power to diminish his love toward us. Troubles
Romans8:36 even though it is written: All day long we face death threats for your sake
Romans8:37 Yet even in the midst of all these things
Romans8:38 So now I live with the confidence that there is nothing in the universe with the power to separate us from God’s love. I’m convinced that his love will triumph over death
Romans8:39 There is no power above us or beneath us—no power that could ever be found in the universe that can distance us from God’s passionate love

Romans13:2 So to resist authority is to resist the divine order of God

2Corinthians11:3 But now I’m afraid that just as Eve was deceived by the serpent’s clever lies
2Corinthians11:4 For you seem to gladly tolerate anyone who comes to you preaching a pseudo-Jesus
2Corinthians11:5 Now
2Corinthians11:6 For although I may not be a polished or eloquent speaker
2Corinthians11:7 Have I committed a sin by degrading myself to dignify you? Was I wrong to preach the gospel of God to you free of charge?
2Corinthians11:8 I received ample financial support from other churches just so that I could freely serve you.
2Corinthians11:9 Remember
2Corinthians11:10 As the reality of Christ lives within me
2Corinthians11:11 Why? Is it because I have no love for you? God knows how much I love you!
2Corinthians11:12 But in order to eliminate the opportunity for those “super-apostles” to boast that their ministry is on the same level as ours I will continue this practice.
2Corinthians11:13 For they are not true apostles but deceitful ministers who masquerade as “special apostles” of the Anointed One.
2Corinthians11:14 That doesn’t surprise us
2Corinthians11:15 So it’s no wonder his servants also go about pretending to be ministers of righteousness. But in the end they will be exposed and get exactly what they deserve.
2Corinthians11:16 So I repeat. Let no one think that I’m a fool. But if you do
2Corinthians11:17 Of course
2Corinthians11:18 For since many love to boast about their worldly achievements
2Corinthians11:19 And since you are so smart and so wise to gladly put up with the foolishness of others
2Corinthians11:20 You actually allow these imposters to put you into bondage
2Corinthians11:21 I must admit
2Corinthians11:22 Are these “super-apostles” of yours Hebrews? I am too. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? Me too!
2Corinthians11:23 Are they servants of the Anointed One? I’m beside myself when I speak this way
2Corinthians11:24 Five times I’ve received thirty-nine lashes from the Jewish leaders.
2Corinthians11:25 Three times I experienced being beaten with rods. Once they stoned me. Three times I’ve been shipwrecked; for an entire night and a day I was adrift in the open sea.
2Corinthians11:26 In my difficult travels I’ve faced many dangerous situations: perilous rivers
2Corinthians11:27 I’ve toiled to the point of exhaustion and gone through many sleepless nights. I’ve frequently been deprived of food and water
2Corinthians11:28 And besides these painful circumstances
2Corinthians11:29 I am not aloof
2Corinthians11:30 If boasting is necessary
2Corinthians11:31 The God and Father of the Lord Jesus
2Corinthians11:32 Once
2Corinthians11:33 but I was stuffed in a basket and lowered down through a window and managed to escape.

Galatians1:1 From Paul
Galatians1:2 All the brothers and sisters join with me as I write this letter to the churches throughout the region of central Turkey.
Galatians1:3 May God’s undeserved kindness and total well-being that flow from our Father God and from the Lord Jesus be yours.
Galatians1:4 He’s the Anointed One who offered himself as the sacrifice for our sins! He has rescued us from this evil world system and set us free
Galatians1:5 May all the glory be to God alone
Galatians1:6 I am shocked over how quickly you have strayed away from the One who called you in the grace of Christ. I’m astounded that you now embrace a distorted gospel!
Galatians1:7 That is a fake “gospel” that is simply not true. There is only one gospel—the good news of Christ! Yet you have allowed those who mingle law with grace to confuse you.
Galatians1:8 But even if we or an angel from heaven should preach a gospel different than the one we preached to you
Galatians1:9 Let me make it clear: Anyone
Galatians1:10 I’m obviously not trying to flatter you or water down my message to be popular with men
Galatians1:11 Beloved ones
Galatians1:12 No one taught me this revelation
Galatians1:13 By now you have heard stories of how severely I harassed and persecuted Christians and how systematically I endeavored to destroy God’s church
Galatians1:14 My zeal and passion for the doctrines of Judaism distinguished me among my people
Galatians1:15 But then God called me by his grace
Galatians1:16 He was pleased to unveil his Son in me so that I would proclaim him to the peoples of the world. After I had this encounter
Galatians1:17 And I had no desire to run to Jerusalem and try to impress those who had become apostles before me. Instead
Galatians1:18 I remained there for three years until I eventually went up to Jerusalem
Galatians1:19 The only other apostle I met during that time was Jacob
Galatians1:20 Everything I’m describing to you I confess before God to be the absolute truth.
Galatians1:21 After my stay in Jerusalem
Galatians1:22 but I remained unknown to the churches in Judea.
Galatians1:23 The only thing they heard about me was this: “Our former enemy
Galatians1:24 Because of the transformation that took place in my life
Galatians2:1 Fourteen years later
Galatians2:2 God gave me a clear revelation to go and confer with the other apostles concerning the message of grace I was preaching to the gentiles. I spoke privately with those who were viewed as senior leaders of the church
Galatians2:3 Even though Titus was a Syrian
Galatians2:4 I met with them privately because false “brothers” had been secretly smuggled into church meetings. They were sent to spy on the wonderful freedom that we have in Jesus Christ. Their agenda was to bring us back into the bondage of religion.
Galatians2:5 But you must know that we did not submit to their religious shackles
Galatians2:6 Even those most influential among the brothers were not able to add anything to my message. Who they are before men makes no difference to me
Galatians2:7 So they recognized that I was entrusted with taking the gospel to the gentiles just as Peter was entrusted with taking it to the Jews.
Galatians2:8 For the same God who empowered Peter’s apostolic ministry to the Jews also flowed through me as an apostle to those who are gentiles.
Galatians2:9 When they all recognized this grace operating in my ministry
Galatians2:10 They simply requested one thing of me: that I would remember the poor and needy
Galatians2:11 When Peter visited Antioch
Galatians2:12 He enjoyed eating with the gentile believers who didn’t keep the Jewish customs—up until the time Jacob’s Jewish friends arrived from Jerusalem. When he saw them
Galatians2:13 And so
Galatians2:14 So when I realized they were acting inconsistently with the revelation of the gospel
Galatians2:15 Although we’re Jews by birth and not gentile “sinners
Galatians2:16 we know that no one receives God’s perfect righteousness as a reward for keeping the law
Galatians2:17 If we are those who desire to be righteous through our union with the Anointed One
Galatians2:18 For if I start over and reconstruct the old religious system that I had torn down with the message of grace
Galatians2:19 For through the law I died to the law
Galatians2:20 My old identity has been co-crucified with Christ and no longer lives. And now the essence of this new life is no longer mine
Galatians2:21 So that is why I don’t view God’s grace as something peripheral. For if keeping the law could release God’s righteousness to us
Galatians3:1 What has happened to you foolish Galatians? Who has put you under an evil spell? Did God not open your eyes to see the meaning of Jesus’ crucifixion? Was he not revealed to you as the crucified one?
Galatians3:2 So answer me this: Did the Holy Spirit come to you as a reward for keeping Jewish laws? No
Galatians3:3 Your new life began when the Holy Spirit gave you a new birth. Why then would you so foolishly turn from living in the Spirit by trying to finish by your own works?
Galatians3:4 Have you endured so many trials and persecutions for nothing?
Galatians3:5 Let me ask you again: What does the lavish supply of the Holy Spirit in your life and the miracles of God’s tremendous power have to do with you keeping religious laws? The Holy Spirit is poured out upon us through the revelation and power of faith!
Galatians3:6 Abraham
Galatians3:7 So the true children of Abraham have the same faith as their father!
Galatians3:8 And the Scripture prophesied that on the basis of faith God would declare gentiles to be righteous. God announced the good news ahead of time to Abraham: “Through your example of faith
Galatians3:9 And so the blessing of Abraham’s faith is now our blessing too!
Galatians3:10 But if you rely on works of keeping the law for salvation
Galatians3:11 It is obvious that no one achieves the righteousness of God by attempting to keep the law
Galatians3:12 But keeping the law does not require faith
Galatians3:13 Yet
Galatians3:14 Jesus Christ dissolved the curse from our lives
Galatians3:15 Beloved friends
Galatians3:16 Remember the royal proclamation God spoke over Abraham and to Abraham’s child? God said that his promises were made to pass on to Abraham’s “Child
Galatians3:17 This means that the covenant between God and Abraham was fulfilled in Messiah and cannot be altered. Yet the written law was not even given to Moses until 430 years after God had “signed” his contract with Abraham! The law
Galatians3:18 
Galatians3:19 Why then was the law given at all? It was given alongside the promise to show people their sins. But the law was designed to last only until the coming of the “Seed
Galatians3:20 Now
Galatians3:21 Since that’s true
Galatians3:22 But the Scriptures make it clear that the whole world is imprisoned by sin! This was so the promise would be given through faith to people who believe in Jesus Christ.
Galatians3:23 So until the revelation of faith for salvation was released
Galatians3:24 The law was our guardian until Christ came so that we would be saved by faith.
Galatians3:25 But now that faith has come we are no longer under the guardian of the law.
Galatians3:26 You have all become true children of God by faith in Jesus Christ!
Galatians3:27 Faith immersed you into Christ
Galatians3:28 And we no longer see each other in our former state —Jew or non-Jew
Galatians3:29 And if you belong to Christ

Galatians4:2 For until the time appointed by the father

Galatians4:7 Now we’re no longer living like slaves under the law

Galatians4:12 Beloved ones

Galatians4:14 And yet you were so kind to me and did not despise me in my weakness
Galatians4:15 Some of you were even willing

Galatians4:25 For “Hagar” represents the law given at Mount Sinai in Arabia. The “Hagar” metaphor corresponds to the earthly Jerusalem of today who are currently in bondage.
Galatians4:26 In contrast
Galatians4:27 For it is written: “Burst forth with gladness
Galatians4:28 Dear friends

Galatians4:31 It’s now so obvious! We’re not the children of the slave woman; we’re the supernatural sons of the freewoman— sons of grace!

Galatians6:13 Not even those who are circumcised keep every detail of the written law. Yet they push you to be circumcised so that they can boast that you have become like them.
Galatians6:14 May my only boast be found in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. In him I have been crucified to this natural realm; and the natural realm is dead to me and no longer dominates my life.
Galatians6:15 Whether a man is circumcised or uncircumcised is meaningless to me. What really matters is the transforming power of this new creation life.
Galatians6:16 And all those who live in agreement with this standard will have true peace and God’s delight

Ephesians1:2 I’m writing this letter to all the devoted believers who have been made holy by being one with Jesus

Ephesians1:4 And in love he chose us before he laid the foundation of the universe! Because of his great love
Ephesians1:5 For it was always in his perfect plan to adopt us as his delightful children
Ephesians1:6 
Ephesians1:7 Since we are now joined to Christ
Ephesians1:8 This superabundant grace is already powerfully working in us
Ephesians1:9 And through the revelation of the Anointed One
Ephesians1:10 And because of God’s unfailing purpose
Ephesians1:11 Through our union with Christ we too have been claimed by God as his own inheritance. Before we were even born
Ephesians1:12 God’s purpose was that we Jews
Ephesians1:13 And because of him
Ephesians1:14 He is given to us like an engagement ring
Ephesians1:15 Because of this
Ephesians1:16 my heart is always full and overflowing with thanks to God for you as I constantly remember you in my prayers.
Ephesians1:17 I pray that the Father of glory
Ephesians1:18 I pray that the light of God will illuminate the eyes of your imagination
Ephesians1:19 I pray that you will continually experience the immeasurable greatness of God’s power made available to you through faith. Then your lives will be an advertisement of this immense power as it works through you! This is the mighty power
Ephesians1:20 that was released when God raised Christ from the dead and exalted him to the place of highest honor and supreme authority in the heavenly realm!
Ephesians1:21 And now he is exalted as first above every ruler
Ephesians1:22 And he alone is the leader and source of everything needed in the church. God has put everything beneath the authority of Jesus Christ and has given him the highest rank above all others.

Ephesians2:1 And his fullness fills you
Ephesians2:2 It wasn’t that long ago that you lived in the religion
Ephesians2:3 The corruption that was in us from birth was expressed through the deeds and desires of our self-life. We lived by whatever natural cravings and thoughts our minds dictated
Ephesians2:4 But God still loved us with such great love. He is so rich in compassion and mercy.
Ephesians2:5 Even when we were dead and doomed in our many sins
Ephesians2:6 He raised us up with Christ the exalted One
Ephesians2:7 Throughout the coming ages we will be the visible display of the infinite riches of his grace and kindness
Ephesians2:8 For by grace you have been saved by faith. Nothing you did could ever earn this salvation
Ephesians2:9 So no one will ever be able to boast
Ephesians2:10 We have become his poetry
Ephesians2:11 So don’t forget that you were not born as Jews and were uncircumcised (circumcision itself is just a work of man’s hands); you had none of the Jewish covenants and laws; you were foreigners to Israel’s incredible heritage; you were without the covenants and prophetic promises of the Messiah
Ephesians2:12 
Ephesians2:13 Yet look at you now! Everything is new! Although you were once distant and far away from God
Ephesians2:14 Our reconciling “Peace” is Jesus! He has made Jew and non-Jew one in Christ. By dying as our sacrifice
Ephesians2:15 Ethnic hatred has been dissolved by the crucifixion of his precious body on the cross. The legal code that stood condemning every one of us has now been repealed by his command. His triune essence has made peace between us by starting over—forming one new race of humanity
Ephesians2:16 Two have now become one
Ephesians2:17 For the Messiah has come to preach this sweet message of peace to you
Ephesians2:18 And now
Ephesians2:19 So
Ephesians2:20 You are rising like the perfectly fitted stones of the temple; and your lives have been built up together upon the foundation laid by the apostles and prophets
Ephesians2:21 This entire building is under construction and is continually growing under his supervision until it rises up completed as the holy temple of the Lord himself.
Ephesians2:22 This means that God is transforming each one of you into the Holy of Holies
Ephesians3:1 Beloved friends
Ephesians3:2 so that you will hear the gospel that God has entrusted to me to share with you.
Ephesians3:3 For this wonderful mystery
Ephesians3:4 so that whenever you read it you will be able to understand my revelation and insight into the secret mystery of the Messiah.
Ephesians3:5 There has never been a generation that has been given the detailed understanding of this glorious and divine mystery until now. He kept it a secret until this generation. God is revealing it only now to his sacred apostles and prophets by the Holy Spirit.
Ephesians3:6 Here’s the secret: The gospel of grace has made you
Ephesians3:7 I have been made a messenger of this wonderful news by the gift of grace that works through me. Even though I am the least significant of all his holy believers
Ephesians3:8 
Ephesians3:9 My passion is to enlighten every person to this divine mystery. It was hidden for ages past until now
Ephesians3:10 The purpose of this was to unveil before every throne and rank of angelic orders in the heavenly realm God’s full and diverse wisdom revealed through the church.
Ephesians3:11 This perfectly wise plan was destined from eternal ages and fulfilled completely in our Lord Jesus Christ
Ephesians3:12 we have boldness through him
Ephesians3:13 My dear friends
Ephesians3:14 So I kneel humbly in awe before the Father of our Lord Jesus
Ephesians3:15 the perfect Father of every father and child in heaven and on the earth.
Ephesians3:16 And I pray that he would unveil within you the unlimited riches of his glory and favor until supernatural strength floods your innermost being with his divine might and explosive power.
Ephesians3:17 Then
Ephesians3:18 Then you will be empowered to discover what every holy one experiences—the great magnitude of the astonishing love of Christ in all its dimensions. How deeply intimate and far-reaching is his love! How enduring and inclusive it is! Endless love beyond measurement that transcends our understanding—this extravagant love pours into you until you are filled to overflowing with the fullness of God!
Ephesians3:19 
Ephesians3:20 Never doubt God’s mighty power to work in you and accomplish all this. He will achieve infinitely more than your greatest request
Ephesians3:21 Now we offer up to God all the glorious praise that rises from every church in every generation through Jesus Christ—and all that will yet be manifest through time and eternity. Amen!

Ephesians4:2 With tender humility and quiet patience
Ephesians4:3 Be faithful to guard the sweet harmony of the Holy Spirit among you in the bonds of peace
Ephesians4:4 being one body and one spirit
Ephesians4:5 For the Lord God is one
Ephesians4:6 And he is the perfect Father who leads us all
Ephesians4:7 And he has generously given each one of us supernatural grace
Ephesians4:8 This is why he says: “He ascends into the heavenly heights taking his many captured ones with him
Ephesians4:9 He “ascended” means that he returned to heaven
Ephesians4:10 The same one who descended is also the one who ascended above the heights of heaven
Ephesians4:11 And he has appointed some with grace to be apostles
Ephesians4:12 And their calling is to nurture and prepare all the holy believers to do their own works of ministry
Ephesians4:13 These grace ministries will function until we all attain oneness into the faith
Ephesians4:14 And then our immaturity will end! And we will not be easily shaken by trouble
Ephesians4:15 But instead we will remain strong and always sincere in our love as we express the truth. All our direction and ministries will flow from Christ and lead us deeper into him
Ephesians4:16 For his “body” has been formed in his image and is closely joined together and constantly connected as one. And every member has been given divine gifts to contribute to the growth of all; and as these gifts operate effectively throughout the whole body
Ephesians4:17 So with the wisdom given to me from the Lord I say: You should not live like the unbelievers around you who walk in their empty delusions.
Ephesians4:18 Their corrupted logic has been clouded because their hearts are so far from God—their blinded understanding and deep-seated moral darkness keeps them from the true knowledge of God.
Ephesians4:19 Because of spiritual apathy
Ephesians4:20 But this is not the way of life that Christ has unfolded within you.
Ephesians4:21 If you have really experienced the Anointed One
Ephesians4:22 And he has taught you to let go of the lifestyle of the ancient man
Ephesians4:23 Now it’s time to be made new by every revelation that’s been given to you.
Ephesians4:24 And to be transformed as you embrace the glorious Christ-within as your new life and live in union with him! For God has re-created you all over again in his perfect righteousness
Ephesians4:25 So discard every form of dishonesty and lying so that you will be known as one who always speaks the truth
Ephesians4:26 But don’t let the passion of your emotions lead you to sin! Don’t let anger control you or be fuel for revenge
Ephesians4:27 Don’t give the slanderous accuser
Ephesians4:28 If any one of you has stolen from someone else
Ephesians4:29 And never let ugly or hateful words come from your mouth
Ephesians4:30 The Holy Spirit of God has sealed you in Jesus Christ until you experience your full salvation. So never grieve the Spirit of God or take for granted his holy influence in your life.
Ephesians4:31 Lay aside bitter words
Ephesians4:32 But instead be kind and affectionate toward one another. Has God graciously forgiven you? Then graciously forgive one another in the depths of Christ’s love.
Ephesians5:1 Be imitators of God in everything you do
Ephesians5:2 And continue to walk surrendered to the extravagant love of Christ
Ephesians5:3 And have nothing to do with sexual immorality
Ephesians5:4 Guard your speech. Forsake obscenities and worthless insults; these are nonsensical words that bring disgrace and are unnecessary. Instead
Ephesians5:5 For it has been made clear to you already that the kingdom of God cannot be accessed by anyone who is guilty of sexual sin
Ephesians5:6 Don’t be fooled by those who speak their empty words and deceptive teachings telling you otherwise. This is what brings God’s anger upon the rebellious!
Ephesians5:7 Don’t listen to them or live like them at all.
Ephesians5:8 Once your life was full of sin’s darkness
Ephesians5:9 And the supernatural fruits of his light will be seen in you—goodness
Ephesians5:10 Then you will learn to choose what is beautiful to our Lord.

Philippians1:1 From Paul and Timothy
Philippians1:2 May the blessings of divine grace and supernatural peace that flow from God our wonderful Father
Philippians1:3 My prayers for you are full of praise to God as I give him thanks for you with great joy! I’m so grateful for our union
Philippians1:4 
Philippians1:5 and our enduring partnership that began the first time I presented to you the gospel.
Philippians1:6 I pray with great faith for you
Philippians1:7 It’s no wonder I pray with such confidence
Philippians1:8 Only God knows how much I dearly love you with the tender affection of Jesus
Philippians1:9 I continue to pray for your love to grow and increase beyond measure
Philippians1:10 This will enable you to choose the most excellent way of all —becoming pure and without offense until the unveiling of Christ.
Philippians1:11 And you will be filled completely with the fruits of righteousness that are found in Jesus
Philippians1:12 I want you to know
Philippians1:13 For now the elite Roman guards and government officials overseeing my imprisonment have plainly recognized that I am here because of my love for the Anointed One.
Philippians1:14 And what I’m going through has actually caused many believers to become even more courageous in the Lord and to be bold and passionate to preach the Word of God
Philippians1:15 It’s true that there are some who preach Christ out of competition and controversy
Philippians1:16 because they know I’ve been destined for the purpose of defending the revelation of God.
Philippians1:17 Those who preach Christ with ambition and competition are insincere—they just want to add to the hardships of my imprisonment.
Philippians1:18 Yet in spite of all of this I am overjoyed! For what does it matter as long as Christ is being preached? If they preach him with mixed motives or with genuine love
Philippians1:19 because I know that the lavish supply of the Spirit of Jesus
Philippians1:20 No matter what
Philippians1:21 My true life is the Anointed One
Philippians1:22 So here’s my dilemma: Each day I live means bearing more fruit in my ministry; yet I fervently long to be liberated from this body and joined fully to Christ. That would suit me fine
Philippians1:23 
Philippians1:24 
Philippians1:25 Yet deep in my heart I’m confident that I will be spared so I can add to your joy and further strengthen and mature your faith.
Philippians1:26 When I am freed to come to you
Philippians1:27 Whatever happens
Philippians1:28 And then you will never be shaken or intimidated by the opposition that rises up against us. Your courage will prove to be a sure sign from God of their coming destruction.
Philippians1:29 For God has graciously given you the privilege not only to believe in Christ
Philippians1:30 For you have been called by him to endure the conflict in the same way I have endured it—for you know I’m not giving up.

Philippians2:3 Be free from pride-filled opinions

Philippians2:9 Because of that obedience

Philippians2:20 Timothy is like no other. He carries the same passion for your welfare that I carry in my heart.

Philippians2:23 After I see what transpires with me he’s the one I will send to you to bless you.

Philippians3:1 My beloved ones
Philippians3:2 beware of those religious hypocrites who teach that you should be circumcised to please God.
Philippians3:3 For we have already experienced “heart-circumcision
Philippians3:4 It’s true that I once relied on all that I had become. I had a reason to boast and impress people with my accomplishments—more than others—for my pedigree was impeccable.
Philippians3:5 I was born a true Hebrew of the heritage of Israel as the son of a Jewish man from the tribe of Benjamin. I was circumcised eight days after my birth and was raised in the strict tradition of Orthodox Judaism
Philippians3:6 And concerning the righteousness of the Torah
Philippians3:7 Yet all of the accomplishments that I once took credit for
Philippians3:8 To truly know him meant letting go of everything from my past and throwing all my boasting on the garbage heap. It’s all like a pile of manure to me now
Philippians3:9 My passion is to be consumed with him and not cling to my own “righteousness” based in keeping the written Law. My only “righteousness” will be his
Philippians3:10 And I continually long to know the wonders of Jesus and to experience the overflowing power of his resurrection working in me. I will be one with him in his sufferings and become like him in his death.
Philippians3:11 Only then will I be able to experience complete oneness with him in his resurrection from the realm of death.
Philippians3:12 I admit that I haven’t yet acquired the absolute fullness that I’m pursuing
Philippians3:13 I don’t depend on my own strength to accomplish this; however I do have one compelling focus: I forget all of the past as I fasten my heart to the future instead.
Philippians3:14 I run straight for the divine invitation of reaching the heavenly goal and gaining the victory-prize through the anointing of Jesus.
Philippians3:15 So let all who are fully mature have this same passion
Philippians3:16 And let us all advance together to reach this victory-prize
Philippians3:17 My beloved friends
Philippians3:18 For there are many who live by different standards. As I’ve warned you many times (I weep as I write these words)
Philippians3:19 doom awaits them. Their god has possessed them and made them mute. Their boast is in their shameful lifestyles and their minds are in the dirt.
Philippians3:20 But we are a colony of heaven on earth as we cling tightly to our life-giver
Philippians3:21 who will transform our humble bodies and transfigure us into the identical likeness of his glorified body. And using his matchless power

Philippians4:7 then God’s wonderful peace that transcends human understanding

Philippians4:9 Put into practice the example of all that you have heard from me or seen in my life and the God of peace will be with you in all things.

Philippians4:11 I’m not telling you this because I’m in need
Philippians4:12 I know what it means to lack
Philippians4:13

Colossians1:17 He existed before anything was made

Colossians2:1 I wish you could know how much I have struggled for you and for the church in Laodicea
Colossians2:2 I am contending for you that your hearts will be wrapped in the comfort of heaven and woven together into love’s fabric. This will give you access to all the riches of God as you experience the revelation of God’s great mystery—Christ.
Colossians2:3 For our spiritual wealth is in him
Colossians2:4 I want you to know this so that no one will come and lead you into error through their persuasive arguments and clever words.
Colossians2:5 Even though I’m separated from you geographically
Colossians2:6 In the same way you received Jesus our Lord and Messiah by faith
Colossians2:7 Your spiritual roots go deeply into his life as you are continually infused with strength
Colossians2:8 Beware that no one distracts you or intimidates you in their attempt to lead you away from Christ’s fullness by pretending to be full of wisdom when they’re filled with endless arguments of human logic. For they operate with humanistic and clouded judgments based on the mindset of this world system
Colossians2:9 For he is the complete fullness of deity living in human form.
Colossians2:10 And our own completeness is now found in him. We are completely filled with God as Christ’s fullness overflows within us. He is the Head of every kingdom and authority in the universe!
Colossians2:11 Through our union with him we have experienced circumcision of heart. All of the guilt and power of sin has been cut away and is now extinct because of what Christ
Colossians2:12 For we’ve been buried with him into his death. Our “baptism into death” also means we were raised with him when we believed in God’s resurrection power
Colossians2:13 This “realm of death” describes our former state
Colossians2:14 He canceled out every legal violation we had on our record and the old arrest warrant that stood to indict us. He erased it all—our sins
Colossians2:15 Then Jesus made a public spectacle of all the powers and principalities of darkness
Colossians2:16 So why would you allow anyone to judge you because of what you eat or drink
Colossians2:17 All of these were but a prophetic shadow and the evidence of what would be fulfilled
Colossians2:18 Don’t let anyone disqualify you from your prize! Don’t let their pretended sincerity fool you as they deliberately lead you into their initiation of angel worship. For they take pleasure in pretending to be experts of something they know nothing about. Their reasoning is meaningless and comes only from their own opinions.
Colossians2:19 They refuse to take hold of the true source. But we receive directly from him
Colossians2:20 For you were included in the death of Christ and have died with him to the religious system and powers of this world. Don’t retreat back to being bullied by the standards and opinions of religion—
Colossians2:21 for example
Colossians2:22 These are the doctrines of men and corrupt customs that are worthless to help you spiritually.
Colossians2:23 For though they may appear to possess the promise of wisdom in their submission to God through the deprivation of their physical bodies

2Thessalonians1:4 We point to you as an example of unwavering faith for all the churches of God. We boast about how you continue to demonstrate unflinching endurance through all the persecutions and painful trials you are experiencing.
2Thessalonians1:5 All of this proves that God’s judgment is always perfect and is intended to make you worthy of inheriting the kingdom of God

2Thessalonians1:7 and give rest to the troubled

2Thessalonians2:4 He is the opposing counterpart who exalts himself over everything that is called “God” or is worshiped and who sits enthroned in God’s temple and makes himself out to be a god.
2Thessalonians2:5 Don’t you remember that when I was with you I went over all these things?
2Thessalonians2:6 Now you are aware of the ruling power so that he may be fully revealed when his time comes.
2Thessalonians2:7 For the mystery of lawlessness is already active
2Thessalonians2:8 Then the “outlaw” will be openly revealed
2Thessalonians2:9 The presence of the “outlaw” is apparent by the activity of Satan
2Thessalonians2:10 and every form of evil deception in order to deceive those who are perishing because they rejected the love of the truth that would lead them to being saved.
2Thessalonians2:11 Because of this
2Thessalonians2:12 So then all who found their pleasure in unrighteousness and did not believe the truth will be judged.
2Thessalonians2:13 We always have to thank God for you
2Thessalonians2:14 To this end he handpicked you for salvation through the gospel so that you would have the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
2Thessalonians2:15 So then
2Thessalonians2:16 Now may the Lord Jesus Christ and our Father God
2Thessalonians2:17 encourage your hearts and inspire you with strength to always do and speak what is good and beautiful in his eyes.

2Thessalonians3:5 Now may the Lord move your hearts into a greater understanding of God’s pure love for you and into Christ’s steadfast endurance.

Hebrews3:12 So search your hearts every day
Hebrews3:13 This is the time to encourage each other to never be stubborn or hardened by sin’s deceitfulness.
Hebrews3:14 For we are mingled with the Messiah
Hebrews3:15 For again

Hebrews4:1 Now the promise of entering into God’s rest is still for us today. So we must be extremely careful to ensure that we all embrace the fullness of that promise and not fail to experience it.
Hebrews4:2 For we have heard the good news of deliverance just as they did
Hebrews4:3 For those of us who believe
Hebrews4:4 for it says in the Scriptures
Hebrews4:5 And again
Hebrews4:6 Those who first heard the good news of deliverance failed to enter into that realm of faith’s rest because of their unbelieving hearts. Yet the fact remains that we still have the opportunity to enter into the faith-rest life and experience the fulfillment of the promise!
Hebrews4:7 For God still has ordained a day for us to enter into called “Today.” For it was long afterwards that God repeated it in David’s words
Hebrews4:8 Now if this promise of “rest” was fulfilled when Joshua brought the people into the land
Hebrews4:9 So we conclude that there is still a full and complete Sabbath-rest waiting for believers to experience.
Hebrews4:10 As we enter into God’s faith-rest life we cease from our own works
Hebrews4:11 So then we must be eager to experience this faith-rest life

Hebrews7:1 Melchizedek’s name means “king of righteousness.” He was the King of Peace
Hebrews7:2 
Hebrews7:3 This Melchizedek has no father or mother
Hebrews7:4 Now let me show you proof of how great this Melchizedek is: – Even though Abraham was the most honored and favored patriarch of all God’s chosen ones
Hebrews7:5 It would be understandable if Melchizedek had been a Jewish priest
Hebrews7:6 But Melchizedek was not Abraham’s Jewish relative
Hebrews7:7 And no one could deny the fact that the one who has the power to impart a blessing is superior to the one who receives it. –
Hebrews7:8 Although the Jewish priests received tithes
Hebrews7:9 It could even be said that Levi
Hebrews7:10 For although Levi was yet unborn
Hebrews7:11 If any of the Levitical priests who served under the law had the power to bring us into perfection
Hebrews7:12 And furthermore
Hebrews7:13 Yet the One these things all point to
Hebrews7:14 for we all know that our Lord didn’t descend from the tribe of Levi
Hebrews7:15 And all this is made even clearer if there was another King-Priest raised up with the rank of Melchizedek.
Hebrews7:16 This King-Priest did not arise because of a genealogical right under the law to be a priest
Hebrews7:17 For it says in the Psalms
Hebrews7:18 The old order of priesthood has been set aside as weak and powerless.

Hebrews8:1 Now this is the crowning point of what we are saying: We have a magnificent King-Priest who ministers for us at the right hand of God. He is enthroned with honor next to the throne of the Majesty on high.
Hebrews8:2 He serves in the holy sanctuary in the true heavenly tabernacle set up by God
Hebrews8:3 Since every high priest is appointed to offer both gifts and sacrifices
Hebrews8:4 But since he didn’t qualify to be an earthly priest
Hebrews8:5 The priests on earth serve in a temple that is but a copy modeled after the heavenly sanctuary; a shadow of the reality. For when Moses began to construct the tabernacle God warned him and said
Hebrews8:6 But now Jesus the Messiah has accepted a priestly ministry which far surpasses theirs
Hebrews8:7 For if that first covenant had been faultless no one would have needed a second one to replace it.
Hebrews8:8 But God revealed the defect and limitation of the first when he said to his people
Hebrews8:9 It will be an entirely different covenant than the one I made with their fathers when I led them by my hand out of Egypt. For they did not remain faithful to my covenant
Hebrews8:10 For here is the covenant I will one day establish with the people of Israel: I will embed my laws within their thoughts and fasten them onto their hearts. I will be their loyal God and they will be my loyal people.
Hebrews8:11 And the result of this will be that everyone will know me as Lord! There will be no need at all to teach their fellow-citizens or brothers by saying
Hebrews8:12 For I will demonstrate my mercy to them and will forgive their evil deeds
Hebrews8:13 This proves that by establishing this new covenant the first is now obsolete
Hebrews9:1 Now in the first covenant there were specific rules for worship including a sanctuary on earth to worship in.
Hebrews9:2 When you entered the tabernacle you would first come into the holy chamber where you would find the lampstand and the bread of his presence on the fellowship table.
Hebrews9:3 Then as you pass through the next curtain you would enter the innermost chamber called
Hebrews9:4 It contained the golden altar of incense and the ark of covenant mercy
Hebrews9:5 On top of the lid of the ark were two cherubim
Hebrews9:6 So with this prescribed pattern of worship the priests would routinely go in and out of the first chamber to perform their religious duties.
Hebrews9:7 And the high priest was permitted to enter into the Holiest Sanctuary of All only once a year and he could never enter without first offering sacrificial blood for both his own sins and for the sins of the people.
Hebrews9:8 Now the Holy Spirit uses the symbols of this pattern of worship to reveal that the perfect way of holiness had not yet been unveiled. For as long as the tabernacle stood
Hebrews9:9 it was an illustration that pointed to our present time of fulfillment
Hebrews9:10 For this old pattern of worship was a matter of external rules and rituals concerning food and drink and ceremonial washings which was imposed upon us until the appointed time of heart-restoration had arrived.
Hebrews9:11 But now the Anointed One has become the King-Priest of every wonderful thing that has come. For he serves in a greater
Hebrews9:12 And he has entered once and forever into the Holiest Sanctuary of All
Hebrews9:13 Under the old covenant the blood of bulls
Hebrews9:14 Yet how much more will the sacred blood of the Messiah thoroughly cleanse our consciences! For by the power of the eternal Spirit he has offered himself to God as the perfect Sacrifice that now frees us from our dead works to worship and serve the living God.
Hebrews9:15 So Jesus is the One who has enacted a new covenant with a new relationship with God so that those who accept the invitation will receive the eternal inheritance he has promised to his heirs. For he died to release us from the guilt of the violations committed under the first covenant.
Hebrews9:16 Now a person’s last will and testament can only take effect after one has been proven to have died; otherwise the will cannot be in force while the person who made it is still alive.
Hebrews9:17 
Hebrews9:18 So this is why not even the first covenant was inaugurated without the blood of animals.
Hebrews9:19 For Moses ratified the covenant after he gave the people all the commandments of the law. He took the blood of calves and goats
Hebrews9:20 saying
Hebrews9:21 And later Moses also sprinkled the tabernacle with blood and every utensil and item used in their service of worship.
Hebrews9:22 Actually
Hebrews9:23 And so it was necessary for all the earthly symbols of the heavenly realities to be purified with these animal sacrifices
Hebrews9:24 For the Messiah did not enter into the earthly tabernacle made by men
Hebrews9:25 Under the old system year after year the high priest entered the most holy sanctuary with blood that was not his own. But the Messiah did not need to repeatedly offer himself year after year
Hebrews9:26 for that would mean he must suffer repeatedly ever since the fall of the world. But now he has appeared at the fulfillment of the ages to abolish sin once and for all by the sacrifice of himself!
Hebrews9:27 Every human being is appointed to die once
Hebrews9:28 But when we die we will be face-to-face with Christ

Hebrews10:25 This is not the time to pull away and neglect meeting together
Hebrews10:26 For if we continue to persist in deliberate sin after we have known and received the truth
Hebrews10:27 But this would qualify one for the certain

Hebrews10:29 How much more severely do you suppose a person deserves to be judged who has contempt for God’s Son
Hebrews10:30 For we know him who said
Hebrews10:31 It is the most terrifying thing of all to come under the judgment of the Living God!
Hebrews10:32 Don’t you remember those days right after the Light shined in your hearts? You endured a great marathon season of suffering hardships
Hebrews10:33 And at times you were publicly and shamefully mistreated
Hebrews10:34 You sympathized with those in prison and when all your belongings were confiscated you accepted that violation with joy; convinced that you possess a treasure growing in heaven that could never be taken from you.
Hebrews10:35 So don’t lose your bold

Hebrews10:37 For soon and very soon
