Hebrews11:1 Now faith brings our hopes into reality and becomes the foundation needed to acquire the things we long for. It is all the evidence required to prove what is still unseen.
Hebrews11:2 This testimony of faith is what previous generations were commended for.
Hebrews11:3 Faith empowers us to see that the universe was created and beautifully coordinated by the power of God’s words! He spoke and the invisible realm gave birth to all that is seen.
Hebrews11:4 Faith moved Abel to choose a more acceptable sacrifice to offer God than his brother Cain
Hebrews11:5 Faith translated Enoch from this life and he was taken up into heaven! He never had to experience death; he just disappeared from this world because God promoted him. For before he was translated to the heavenly realm his life had become a pleasure to God.
Hebrews11:6 And without faith living within us it would be impossible to please God. For we come to God in faith knowing that he is real and that he rewards the faith of those who passionately seek him.
Hebrews11:7 Faith opened Noah’s heart to receive revelation and warnings from God about what was coming
Hebrews11:8 Faith motivated Abraham to obey God’s call and leave the familiar to discover the territory he was destined to inherit from God. So he left with only a promise and without even knowing ahead of time where he was going
Hebrews11:9 He lived by faith as an immigrant in his promised land as though it belonged to someone else. He journeyed through the land living in tents with Isaac and Jacob who were persuaded that they were also co-heirs of the same promise.
Hebrews11:10 His eyes of faith were set on the city with unshakable foundations
Hebrews11:11 Sarah’s faith embraced God’s miracle power to conceive even though she was barren and was past the age of childbearing
Hebrews11:12 In fact
Hebrews11:13 These heroes all died still clinging to their faith
Hebrews11:14 For clearly
Hebrews11:15 And if their hearts were still remembering what they left behind
Hebrews11:16 But they couldn’t turn back for their hearts were fixed on what was far greater
Hebrews11:17 Faith operated powerfully in Abraham for when he was put to the test he offered up Isaac. Even though he received God’s promises of descendants
Hebrews11:18 For God had promised
Hebrews11:19 Abraham’s faith made it logical to him that God could raise Isaac from the dead
Hebrews11:20 The power of faith prompted Isaac to impart a blessing to his sons
Hebrews11:21 Jacob worshiped in faith’s reality at the end of his life
Hebrews11:22 Faith inspired Joseph and opened his eyes to see into the future
Hebrews11:23 Faith prompted the parents of Moses at his birth to hide him for three months
Hebrews11:24 Faith enabled Moses to choose God’s will
Hebrews11:25 choosing instead to suffer mistreatment with the people of God. Moses preferred faith’s certainty above the momentary enjoyment of sin’s pleasures.
Hebrews11:26 He found his true wealth in suffering abuse for being anointed
Hebrews11:27 Holding faith’s promise Moses abandoned Egypt and had no fear of Pharaoh’s rage because he persisted in faith as if he had seen God who is unseen.
Hebrews11:28 Faith stirred Moses to perform the rite of Passover and sprinkle lamb’s blood
Hebrews11:29 Faith opened the way for the Hebrews to cross the Red Sea as if on dry land
Hebrews11:30 Faith pulled down Jericho’s walls after the people marched around them for seven days!
Hebrews11:31 Faith provided a way of escape for Rahab the prostitute
Hebrews11:32 And what more could I say to convince you? For there is not enough time to tell you of the faith of Gideon
Hebrews11:33 Through faith’s power they conquered kingdoms and established true justice. Their faith fastened onto their promises and pulled them into reality! It was faith that shut the mouth of lions
Hebrews11:34 put out the power of raging fire
Hebrews11:35 Faith-filled women saw their dead children raised in resurrection power. Yet it was faith that enabled others to endure great atrocities. They were stretched out on the wheel and tortured
Hebrews11:36 Others were mocked and experienced the most severe beating with whips; they were in chains and imprisoned.
Hebrews11:37 Some of these faith champions were brutally killed by stoning
Hebrews11:38 They wandered the earth living in the desert wilderness
Hebrews11:39 These were the true heroes
Hebrews11:40 But now God has invited us to live in something better than what they had—faith’s fullness! This is so that they could be brought to finished perfection alongside of us.
Hebrews12:1 As for us
Hebrews12:2 We look away from the natural realm and we focus our attention and expectation onto Jesus who birthed faith within us and who leads us forward into faith’s perfection. His example is this: Because his heart was focused on the joy of knowing that you would be his
Hebrews12:3 So consider carefully how Jesus faced such intense opposition from sinners who opposed their own souls
Hebrews12:4 After all
Hebrews12:5 And have you forgotten his encouraging words spoken to you as his children? He said
Hebrews12:6 For the Lord’s training of your life is the evidence of his faithful love. And when he draws you to himself
Hebrews12:7 Fully embrace God’s correction as part of your training
Hebrews12:8 We all should welcome God’s discipline as the validation of authentic sonship. For if we have never once endured his correction it only proves we are strangers and not sons.
Hebrews12:9 And isn’t it true that we respect our earthly fathers even though they corrected and disciplined us? Then we should demonstrate an even greater respect for God
Hebrews12:10 Our parents corrected us for the short time of our childhood as it seemed good to them. But God corrects us throughout our lives for our own good
Hebrews12:11 Now all discipline seems to be painful at the time
Hebrews12:12 So be made strong even in your weakness by lifting up your tired hands in prayer and worship. And strengthen your weak knees
Hebrews12:13 for as you keep walking forward on God’s paths all your stumbling ways will be divinely healed!
Hebrews12:14 In every relationship be swift to choose peace over competition
Hebrews12:15 Watch over each other to make sure that no one misses the revelation of God’s grace. And make sure no one lives with a root of bitterness sprouting within them which will only cause trouble and poison the hearts of many.
Hebrews12:16 Be careful that no one among you lives in immorality
Hebrews12:17 And we know that later on when he wanted to inherit his father’s blessing
Hebrews12:18 For we are not coming
Hebrews12:19 We are not those who are being warned by the jarring blast of a trumpet and the thundering voice; the fearful voice that they begged to be silenced.
Hebrews12:20 They couldn’t handle God’s command that said
Hebrews12:21 The astounding phenomena Moses witnessed caused him to shudder with fear and he could only say
Hebrews12:22 By contrast
Hebrews12:23 And as members of the church of the Firstborn all our names have been legally registered as citizens of heaven! And we have come before God who judges all
Hebrews12:24 And we have come to Jesus who established a new covenant with his blood sprinkled upon the mercy seat; blood that continues to speak from heaven
Hebrews12:25 Make very sure that you never refuse to listen to God when he speaks! For the God who spoke on earth from Sinai is the same God who now speaks from heaven. Those who heard him speak his living Word on earth found nowhere to hide
Hebrews12:26 The earth was rocked at the sound of his voice from the mountain
Hebrews12:27 Now this phrase “once and for all” clearly indicates the final removal of things that are shaking
Hebrews12:28 Since we are receiving our rights to an unshakable kingdom we should be extremely thankful and offer God the purest worship that delights his heart as we lay down our lives in absolute surrender
Hebrews12:29 For our God is a holy

Hebrews13:2 And show hospitality to strangers
Hebrews13:3 Identify with those who are in prison as though you were there suffering with them
Hebrews13:4 Honor the sanctity of marriage and keep your vows of purity to one another

Hebrews13:6 So we can say with great confidence: “I know the Lord is for me and I will never be afraid of what people may do to me!”
Hebrews13:7 Don’t forget the example of your spiritual leaders who have spoken God’s messages to you
Hebrews13:8 Jesus
Hebrews13:9 So don’t let anyone lead you astray with all sorts of novel and exotic teachings. It is more beautiful to feast on grace and be inwardly strengthened than to be obsessed with dietary rules which in themselves have no lasting benefit.
Hebrews13:10 We feast on a sacrifice at our spiritual altar
Hebrews13:11 For the high priest carries the blood of animals into the holiest chamber as a sacrifice for sin
Hebrews13:12 And Jesus
Hebrews13:13 So we must arise and join him outside the religious “walls” and bear his disgrace.
Hebrews13:14 For we have no city here on earth to be our permanent home
Hebrews13:15 So we no longer offer up a steady stream of blood sacrifices
Hebrews13:16 We will show mercy to the poor and not miss an opportunity to do acts of kindness for others
Hebrews13:17 Obey your spiritual leaders and recognize their authority
Hebrews13:18 And keep praying for us that we continue to live with a clear conscience
Hebrews13:19 And I especially ask you to pray that God would send me back to you very soon.
Hebrews13:20 Now may the God who brought us peace by raising from the dead our Lord Jesus Christ so that he would be the Great Shepherd of his flock; and by the power of the blood of the eternal covenant
Hebrews13:21 may he work perfection into every part of you giving you all that you need to fulfill your destiny. And may he express through you all that is excellent and pleasing to him through your life-union with Jesus the Anointed One who is to receive all glory forever! Amen!

Hebrews13:23 I want you to know that our brother Timothy is free again and as soon as he arrives here we’ll come together to see you. We extend our greetings to all your leaders and all the holy believers.
Hebrews13:24 The Italian believers also send their greetings.
Hebrews13:25 Now may God’s wonderful grace be poured out upon you all! Amen!

James1:2 My fellow believers
James1:3 For you know that when your faith is tested it stirs up in you the power of endurance.
James1:4 And then as your endurance grows even stronger
James1:5 And if anyone longs to be wise
James1:6 Just make sure you ask empowered by confident faith without doubting that you will receive. For the ambivalent person believes one minute and doubts the next. Being undecided makes you become like the rough seas driven and tossed by the wind. You’re up one minute and tossed down the next.
James1:7 When you are half-hearted and wavering it leaves you unstable. Can you really expect to receive anything from the Lord when you’re in that condition?
James1:8 
James1:9 The believer who is poor still has reasons to boast
James1:10 But those who are rich should boast in how God has brought them low and humbled them
James1:11 For as the scorching heat of the sun causes the petals of the wildflower to fall off and lose its appearance of beauty
James1:12 If your faith remains strong
James1:13 When you are tempted don’t ever say
James1:14 Instead it is each person’s own desires and thoughts that drag them into evil and lure them away into darkness.
James1:15 Evil desires give birth to evil actions. And when sin is fully mature it can murder you!
James1:16 So my friends
James1:17 Every gift God freely gives us is good and perfect
James1:18 God was delighted to give us birth by the truth of his infallible Word so that we would fulfill his chosen destiny for us and become the favorite ones out of all his creation!
James1:19 My dearest brothers and sisters
James1:20 for human anger is never a legitimate tool to promote God’s righteous purpose.
James1:21 So this is why we abandon everything morally impure and all forms of wicked conduct. Instead
James1:22 Don’t just listen to the Word of Truth and not respond to it
James1:23 If you listen to the Word and don’t live out the message you hear
James1:24 You perceive how God sees you in the mirror of the Word
James1:25 But those who set their gaze deeply into the perfecting law of liberty are fascinated by and respond to the truth they hear and are strengthened by it—they experience God’s blessing in all that they do!
James1:26 If someone believes they have a relationship with God but fails to guard his words then his heart is drifting away and his religion is shallow and empty.
James1:27 True spirituality that is pure in the eyes of our Father God is to make a difference in the lives of the orphans

James2:7 Aren’t they the very ones who blaspheme the beautiful name of the One you now belong to?
James2:8 Your calling is to fulfill the royal law of love as given to us in this Scripture: “You must love and value your neighbor as you love and value yourself!” For keeping this law is the noble way to live.
James2:9 But when you show prejudice you commit sin and you violate this royal law of love!
James2:10 For the one who attempts to keep all of the law of Moses but fails in just one point has become guilty of breaking the law in every respect!

James3:1 My dear brothers and sisters
James3:2 We all fail in many areas
James3:3 Horses have bits and bridles in their mouths so that we can control and guide their large body.
James3:4 And the same with mighty ships
James3:5 And so the tongue is a small part of the body yet it carries great power! Just think of how a small flame can set a huge forest ablaze.
James3:6 And the tongue is a fire! It can be compared to the sum total of wickedness and is the most dangerous part of our human body. It corrupts the entire body and is a hellish flame! It releases a fire that can burn throughout the course of human existence.
James3:7 For every wild animal on earth including birds
James3:8 but the tongue is not able to be tamed. It’s a fickle
James3:9 We use our tongue to praise God our Father and then turn around and curse a person who was made in his very image!
James3:10 Out of the same mouth we pour out words of praise one minute and curses the next. My brothers and sisters
James3:11 Would you look for olives hanging on a fig tree or go to pick figs from a grapevine? Is it possible that fresh and bitter water can flow out of the same spring? So neither can a bitter spring produce fresh water.
James3:12 
James3:13 If you consider yourself to be wise and one who understands the ways of God
James3:14 But if there is bitter jealousy or competition hiding in your heart
James3:15 For that has nothing to do with God’s heavenly wisdom but can best be described as the wisdom of this world
James3:16 So wherever jealousy and selfishness are uncovered
James3:17 But the wisdom from above is always pure
James3:18 and it always bears the beautiful harvest of righteousness! Good seeds of wisdom’s fruit will be planted with peaceful acts by those who cherish making peace.

James5:2 Your riches lie rotting
James5:3 and your gold and silver are corroded as a witness against you. You have hoarded up treasure for the last days but it will become a fire to burn your flesh.
James5:4 Listen! Can’t you hear the cries of the laborers over the wages you fraudulently held back from those who worked for you? The cries for justice of those you’ve cheated have reached the ears of the Lord of armies!
James5:5 You have indulged yourselves with every luxury and pleasure this world offers
James5:6 You have condemned and murdered good and innocent people who had no power to defend themselves.

James5:8 So you also

James5:12 Above all we must be those who never need to verify our speech as truthful by swearing by the heavens or the earth or any other oath. But instead we must be so full of integrity that our “Yes” or “No” is convincing enough and we do not stumble into hypocrisy.

Revelation2:4 But I have this against you: you have abandoned the passionate love you had for me at the beginning.

Revelation19:16 On his robe and on his thigh he had inscribed a name: King of kings and Lord of lords.
Revelation19:17 Then I saw an angel standing in the sun
Revelation19:18 Come and devour the flesh of the kings
Revelation19:19 Then I saw the wild beast and the kings of the earth with their armies gathered to wage war against the rider on the white horse and against his armies.
Revelation19:20 The wild beast was captured
Revelation19:21 and their armies were killed by the sharp sword that came from the mouth of the rider on the white horse. And all the birds gorged themselves with their flesh.

Revelation20:2 He seized the dragon
Revelation20:3 The mighty angel threw him into the pit
Revelation20:4 Then I saw thrones
Revelation20:5 This is the first resurrection. (The rest of the dead did not come to life until the thousand years were ended.)

Revelation20:7 After the thousand years are over
Revelation20:8 and will go out and deceive the nations of the four corners of the earth
Revelation20:9 They spread out over the earth and surrounded the camp of God’s holy believers and the beloved city. But fire came down from heaven and completely destroyed them.
Revelation20:10 Then the devil who had deceived them was thrown into the same place with the wild beast and the false prophet—the lake of fire and sulfur—where they will be tormented day and night forever and ever.

Revelation20:12 I saw the dead
Revelation20:13 And the sea gave up the dead souls that were in it. Then death and the underworld gave up their dead
Revelation20:14 Then death and the realm of the dead were cast into the lake of fire
Revelation20:15 And anyone whose name was not recorded in the Book of Life was cast into the lake of fire.

Revelation21:6 Then he said to me

Revelation21:18 The city was pure gold

Revelation21:20 the fifth onyx
Revelation21:21 The twelve gates are twelve pearls—each gate made of one pearl. And the street of the city was pure gold
Revelation21:22 I saw no temple in the city
Revelation21:23 The city has no need for the sun or moon to shine
Revelation21:24 The people will walk by its light and the kings of the earth will bring their wealth into it.
Revelation21:25 Its gates will never be shut by day—standing always open—because there is no night there.
Revelation21:26 People will bring the glory and wealth of the nations into it.
